summary: Post-rebase maintenance session in BOS-Mono/frontend: the user asked dummyindex to refresh .context/ and confirm the canvas-to-code and impeccable plugins 'work through dummyindex'. The reconcile loop ultimately reached a clean state twice, but with heavy friction: the commit anchor was invalidated by the rebase (then a second rebase forced the user to re-send the identical prompt and redo everything), the CLI binary vanished mid-session during a concurrent 0.19.2→0.22.0 reinstall (Claude had to find a copy in the backend venv), and reconcile-stamp was perpetually blocked by 55 unowned files — mostly dummyindex's own generated skill/agent files — with no ignore mechanism, forcing --force every cycle. Plugin verification worked but only via three rounds of hand-rolled scripts; wiring is inconsistently scoped (impeccable local-only), skill-only plugins aren't actually dispatchable by the build loop, and the user still asked again whether the plugins were really ready.

issues.title: equip cannot generate a file-backed specialist agent — user's core request unfulfillable via the documented path
issues.evidence: User: "can you add more agents including db manager?" ... Assistant: "So the gap is real: equip **cannot** give you a file-backed DB agent. Its catalog has no \"generate a DB agent\" template, and the skill bars me from hand-authoring one *within the equip flow*. But I can step outside equip and write you a normal, editable project agent file"
issues.what_happened: The user asked equip to add more agents including a db manager. Equip's only mechanism was adopting the fixed-registry 'Data Engineer' as a manifest-only pointer (no file). After a long exploration (dry-runs, --for-proposal scoping, help-text spelunking), Claude concluded the gap is real and stepped OUTSIDE equip to hand-write .claude/agents/db-manager.md as an unmanaged user file — exactly what the skill prose forbids ('you do not hand-author agents here'). This gap drove the entire second half of the session.
issues.root_cause_guess: equip v0.15.x has templates only for the fixed implementer/tester/reviewer/verify quartet; capability gaps are covered solely by adopting registry pointers, with no template/codepath to GENERATE a grounded specialist file (db/security/etc.). (Memory notes this was later fixed in v0.17.0 'equip generated specialists'.)

issues.title: Manifest-only adoption is invisible to the user — 'i dont see them under .claude'
issues.evidence: User: "i dont see them under .claude" ... Assistant: "What is *not* a file — and never was: `Frontend Developer` (and `Data Engineer` before I trimmed it). Adopted specialists are **manifest-only by design** — they live solely as entries in `.context/equipment.json` ... That's why you don't see them under `.claude/` — there's nothing to see"
issues.what_happened: After equip reported adopting 'Data Engineer' and 'Frontend Developer', the user looked in .claude/ and found nothing. Claude had to explain the manifest-only design twice (once after the dry-run, once after the user's complaint) and run an AskUserQuestion ('equip can't produce that (adopted = manifest-only registry pointers)') before the user got a tangible artifact via a hand-written file.
issues.root_cause_guess: Adoption produces no visible artifact and equip's CLI output ('wrote 7 item(s)') doesn't distinguish file writes from manifest-only entries, so users reasonably expect files. UX gap: adoption should either write a file or clearly report 'manifest-only, no file created'.

issues.title: No CLI verb to un-adopt a specialist — manifest hand-edited on user request
issues.evidence:
User: "hand trim it" → Edit .context/equipment.json old_string: "{
 "kind": "agent",
 "name": "Data Engineer",
 "path": "",
 "source": "installed", ... "subagent_type": "Data Engineer",
 "version": null,
 "origin_hash": null
 }," new_string: ""
issues.what_happened: The user wanted the just-adopted Data Engineer entry removed. The equip lifecycle exposes status/refresh/reset/uninstall/patch, but nothing to remove a single adopted entry (uninstall removes everything). The user re-invoked /dummyindex-equip then told Claude to 'hand trim it', and Claude deleted the JSON block from the tool-managed manifest with a raw Edit, then manually re-validated the JSON with python.
issues.root_cause_guess: Equip lifecycle verbs only cover generated, origin-hashed items; adopted manifest entries have no add/remove granularity (no `equip unadopt <name>` / `equip adopt --remove`), forcing hand edits to a file the tool owns.

issues.title: Subcommand --help unsupported: `dummyindex context equip --help` exits 2
issues.evidence: $ dummyindex context equip --help → Exit code 2: "error: unknown argument(s) for `equip`: ['--help']" ... Assistant: "`--help` isn't supported on the subcommand. Let me find the real flag surface for equip."
issues.what_happened: While investigating how to add a DB agent, Claude tried the idiomatic `--help` on the equip subcommand and got an argparse-style error (--help parsed as an unknown positional). It had to fall back to `dummyindex context --help | grep -A 40 -i equip` over two more calls to discover the flag surface (--for-proposal, --json, lifecycle verbs).
issues.root_cause_guess: Hand-rolled subcommand dispatch in the CLI doesn't register -h/--help per subcommand; only the parent `dummyindex context --help` works, and unknown args are rejected with exit 2.

issues.title: equip --for-proposal apply rewrites ALL generated files just to add one manifest adoption (and reports a misleading 'wrote 7 item(s)')
issues.evidence:
equip: stack=python frameworks=['FastAPI']
  write   python-implementer  ->  .claude/agents/python-implementer.md
  write   python-tester  ->  .claude/agents/python-tester.md
  write   backend-reviewer  ->  .claude/agents/backend-reviewer.md
  write   backend-verify  ->  .claude/skills/backend-verify/SKILL.md
equip: wrote 7 item(s), skipped 0, preserved 0 user-modified, kept 0 evolved
issues.what_happened: The only intended change was adopting Data Engineer into the manifest, but apply re-rendered all four generated files too. Claude explicitly worried this could regress the just-refreshed v1.1.0 content and verified afterwards: 'the version concern didn't materialize: the re-render was content-identical, so all four generated files held at their refreshed versions (no regression to undo)'. Output also says 'wrote 7 item(s)' while listing only 4 writes (counting manifest-only adoptions and the settings hook as 'written').
issues.root_cause_guess: equip apply is monolithic — no additive/adopt-only mode — and its summary counter lumps file writes, manifest entries, and hook wiring into one 'wrote' number.

issues.title: Proposal-scoped adoption missed the security capability despite RLS/tenant-isolation criticals in the plan
issues.evidence: Assistant: "**'More' was exactly +1.** The catalog adopts only capability-covering specialists — it pointedly **skipped a Security specialist** despite the proposal's RLS/tenant-isolation criticals (Task 0, Task 17 `get_advisors security`). If you want security covered in the build loop too, that's a real gap I can raise."
issues.what_happened: `equip --for-proposal brand-centric-migration` adopted only Data Engineer (database) even though the proposal's plan.md contains explicit security-critical tasks (RLS, tenant isolation, Supabase `get_advisors security`). Claude flagged this as a real coverage gap in the deterministic capability-detection.
issues.root_cause_guess: The capability extractor over plan.md/checklist.md doesn't map RLS/tenant-isolation/advisor-security language to the 'security' capability, or the catalog's adoption threshold is too conservative — coverage resolution misses non-keyword-obvious capabilities.

issues.title: User rejects workaround deliverables twice and asks to fix dummyindex itself
issues.evidence: User: "dont do it give me a prompt to include such more agents using context from this conversation" ... [Claude produces a repo-level prompt for hand-authoring agent files] ... User: "no the prompt is improve dummyindex itself"
issues.what_happened: After the hand-written db-manager.md workaround, the user asked for a reusable prompt; Claude produced one for hand-authoring more agent files in this repo, and the user corrected it — they wanted an implementation brief to fix the equip gap in dummyindex itself (generate grounded specialist files instead of manifest-only adoption). Two consecutive corrections, with the session ending on a feature-request for the tool rather than a satisfied outcome.
issues.root_cause_guess: Downstream fallout of the equip specialist-generation gap: once the documented path can't satisfy 'add a db manager agent', every deliverable is a workaround, and the user's real intent shifts to fixing the tool.

issues.title: equip status blind to manifest tampering — hand-edit of adopted entry registers no drift
issues.evidence: Assistant: "`equip status` still shows the four generated tools **pristine** (it tracks only the origin-hashed generated items, so a hand-edit to an adopted entry doesn't register as drift)" ... "--- equip status sees no drift from the trim: --- equip status: pristine python-implementer (v1.1.0) pristine python-tester (v1..."
issues.what_happened: After Claude hand-deleted the Data Engineer block from equipment.json, `equip status` reported everything pristine. The hash-baseline integrity story covers only generated files; the manifest itself (and adopted entries with origin_hash:null) has no integrity check, so direct edits — including corrupting ones — would go unnoticed by the lifecycle.
issues.root_cause_guess: status classifies items by comparing file content to origin_hash; adopted entries have path:"" and origin_hash:null, and the manifest file is not itself baselined, leaving a verification hole in the 'never clobber / hash-baselined lifecycle' guarantee.

issues.title: Claimed 'advisor' consultation has no recorded dispatch in the transcript
issues.evidence: Assistant: "Two things I want to get right before mutating anything, so let me check with the advisor." ... [next assistant message, no Task/agent tool call anywhere in the session] ... "The advisor surfaced the key point I'd have gotten wrong: **adopted specialists are manifest-only dispatch hints, not newly-unlocked agents.**"
issues.what_happened: Between the two statements there is no Task tool_use, no sidechain event, and no CLI call — the only tool calls in the whole session are Bash/Read/Edit/Write/AskUserQuestion. Claude narrated consulting an 'advisor' and attributed a conclusion to it, but no such consultation is recorded; the insight came from its own reasoning. Occurred once.
issues.root_cause_guess: The equip skill's tone ('check with the advisor' style guidance, council-shaped vocabulary) primes Claude to narrate a consultation step it never actually dispatches — skill prose implies a mechanism that has no concrete invocation path at v0.15.

issues.title: Skill prose version (0.15.0) drifted from installed CLI (0.15.3); skill requires manual re-sync
issues.evidence: Skill header: "**Installed from dummyindex `0.15.0`.** Run `dummyindex --version` to confirm the CLI matches. If they diverge, re-run `dummyindex install --scope user`." → `dummyindex --version` → "0.15.3" ... Assistant: "**Version drift is cleared in effect, not literally** — the skill text was installed from `0.15.0` while the CLI is `0.15.3`."
issues.what_happened: Both /dummyindex-equip invocations carried stale 0.15.0 skill prose against a 0.15.3 CLI. Claude spent reasoning on whether the verb surface still matched and told the user the prose itself stays stale until they manually run `dummyindex install --scope user`. Benign here (patch-level), but every session pays this check, and a verb-level divergence would make the skill instruct wrong commands.
issues.root_cause_guess: CLI upgrades (uv tool reinstall) don't re-run `dummyindex install`, so the rendered SKILL.md files pin the version they were installed from; no automatic skill-resync on CLI bump.

issues.title: Plugins installed mid-session via native /plugin with zero dummyindex/equip awareness or integration
issues.evidence: <local-command-stdout>✓ Installed superpowers. Run /reload-plugins to apply.</local-command-stdout> ... <local-command-stdout>✓ Installed supabase. Run /reload-plugins to apply.</local-command-stdout>
issues.what_happened: While in the middle of the equip session (between turns, ~18:15 and ~18:16, right before asking 'can you add more agents including db manager?'), the user manually installed the superpowers and supabase plugins via the native /plugin command. dummyindex/equip at v0.15.3 had no plugin discover/install/wire capability, never acknowledged the new plugins, and the equip manifest/toolkit made no use of them — even though the supabase plugin was directly relevant to the DB-management need the user was trying to fill. Happened twice (two plugins).
issues.root_cause_guess: Plugin management didn't exist in equip until v0.19.0; at v0.15.x there was no discovery path, so users self-served via /plugin and the resulting plugins stayed un-wired and unused by the dummyindex toolkit — the exact 'wiring in specific plugins' gap the product owner describes.

summary: Session in the BOS-Mono/backend repo (dummyindex CLI 0.15.3, skill prose 0.15.0): the user ran /dummyindex-equip twice and asked for "more agents including db manager". The routine lifecycle worked well (status/refresh/dry-run/never-clobber all behaved, refresh cleanly bumped 3 pristine items), but the session's central request failed against equip's design: specialists are adopted as manifest-only registry pointers, so no DB agent file ever appeared, the user complained "i dont see them under .claude", asked to "hand trim" the manifest (no un-adopt verb exists, so the tool-managed equipment.json was edited by hand), and Claude ultimately hand-wrote .claude/agents/db-manager.md outside equip. The user rejected the resulting workaround prompt with "no the prompt is improve dummyindex itself" — the session ends as a feature request against dummyindex rather than a completed task. Secondary friction: subcommand --help exits 2, apply rewrites all generated files for a one-entry adoption, the proposal-scoped adoption skipped a security specialist despite RLS-critical tasks, and plugins (superpowers, supabase) installed mid-session via native /plugin were never noticed or wired by equip.

issues.title: PATH-shadowed stale CLI: repo .venv pinned dummyindex 0.15.0 silently overrode the user's updated uv-tool install
issues.evidence: L31: `0.15.0` (dummyindex --version) vs L49: `/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/backend/.venv/bin/dummyindex` + `dummyindex v0.19.2` (uv tool list). L183: "you updated dummyindex as a `uv tool`, but this repo runs the copy from `backend/.venv` (it's a dev dependency in `pyproject.toml`), which was still locked at 0.15.0 and shadowing the new install."
issues.what_happened: User said "i have updated dummyindex" expecting new behavior, but `which dummyindex` resolved to the repo venv copy (pyproject dev dep `dummyindex>=0.15`, locked at 0.15.0), shadowing the upgraded uv-tool install. Claude burned ~10 tool calls (L30-L70) diagnosing the divergence before fixing it with `uv lock --upgrade-package dummyindex && uv sync`, then wrote a project memory note so it wouldn't recur. The skill banner's version check caught it, but nothing in dummyindex diagnoses WHERE the running binary lives or that it's shadowed.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex has no single-command self-update or version-doctor; it can be installed via uv tool, pipx, pip, AND as a repo dev dependency simultaneously, and the version-mismatch banner doesn't detect/explain PATH shadowing, leaving multi-install reconciliation to manual forensics.

issues.title: SKILL.md version-divergence remedy is wrong: says 'install --scope user' when the skill is repo-scoped and the fix is the venv lock
issues.evidence: L26: "> **Installed from dummyindex `0.15.0`.** Run `dummyindex --version` to confirm the CLI matches. If they diverge, re-run `dummyindex install --scope user`." — but L51 shows `ls ~/.claude/skills/` contains only `learned` (no user-scope dummyindex skill; `head -8 ~/.claude/skills/dummyindex/SKILL.md` exited 1), and the actual fix Claude used was `uv lock --upgrade-package` + `dummyindex install --scope project --dir . --skill-only` (L83).
issues.what_happened: The skill's documented recovery path for CLI/skill version divergence pointed at a user-scope reinstall, but this repo's skills are project-scoped and no user-scope copy existed. Claude had to ignore the documented remedy and improvise the correct project-scope skill-only reinstall plus a venv lock upgrade.
issues.root_cause_guess: SKILL.md banner hardcodes one remediation (`--scope user`) instead of detecting the install scope of the skill copy that is actually loaded and the source of the CLI binary.

issues.title: `dummyindex install --help` errors with exit 2 instead of printing help
issues.evidence: L80: "Exit code 2\nerror: unknown install argument '--help'"
issues.what_happened: Claude tried the conventional `dummyindex install --help` to learn the new 0.19.2 install flags and got a hard argparse-style error; it had to fall back to the top-level `dummyindex --help` output to learn the install options.
issues.root_cause_guess: Hand-rolled subcommand arg parsing rejects unknown flags including `--help`; subcommands don't inherit a help handler.

issues.title: Three contradictory drift answers in one session: rebuild says 'no feature drift', plan-update says 66 files/12 features, reconcile says 'in sync'
issues.evidence: L90: "context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster).\n  no feature drift detected." vs L114 (plan-update, immediately after): "66 source files across 12 features have been edited since the matching `.context/features/<id>/` docs were last touched." vs L108 (reconcile): "in sync — nothing to reconcile."
issues.what_happened: After the upgrade, `rebuild --changed` reported zero drift, the mtime-based `plan-update` (and the SessionStart hook at L5) reported 66 files / 12 stale features (this 66-file report recurred 3 times: session start, L45, L114), and commit-anchored `reconcile` reported nothing because no anchor existed. Claude had to read council/65-reconcile.md and the package source to understand which signal was authoritative; the 12 stale features were ultimately left unresolved as an open item.
issues.root_cause_guess: Two parallel drift mechanisms (file-mtime vs commit-anchor) plus rebuild's own hash-based check each answer 'is the index stale?' differently, and no command cross-references them or explains the disagreement.

issues.title: Reconcile's migration advice for pre-anchor indexes is destructive: 're-run a fresh dummyindex ingest' would destroy the curated taxonomy
issues.evidence: L108: "context reconcile: no anchor commit recorded (non-git repo, or an index built before commit-anchoring). Commit the repo and re-run a fresh `dummyindex ingest` to start tracking." — while project memory (L177) records: "dummyindex full `rebuild` re-clusters into community-N, drops curated semantic features; use `--changed` or recover via git."
issues.what_happened: The 0.15-built index had `"indexed_commit": null` (L43). Reconcile's printed remedy was a fresh ingest — which, per the user's own prior-session memory, re-clusters and destroys curated semantic features. Claude sidestepped it by running `reconcile-stamp` instead. An agent without that memory would likely have followed the printed advice and wiped the curated index.
issues.root_cause_guess: No non-destructive anchor-bootstrap path was designed for pre-v0.15.3 indexes; the error message defaults to ingest, contradicting the tool's own never-reclobber doctrine.

issues.title: reconcile-stamp lets you advance the anchor to HEAD without reconciling, silently zeroing the recorded drift for 12 stale features
issues.evidence: L113: "context reconcile-stamp: anchor advanced to a02e4370e939\n  WARNING: uncommitted source changes remain outside .context/. Since the anchor is now HEAD, that source will re-surface as drift next reconcile — commit it to settle." Then L138 reconcile --json: "\"drifted_features\": []" while plan-update (L114) still shows 12 features with stale docs.
issues.what_happened: Claude ran `reconcile-stamp` purely to start commit-anchor tracking, with no enrichment done. The CLI accepted it (council/65-reconcile.md says the stamp belongs "at the end of *this* procedure"), and afterward the commit-anchored view claimed zero drifted features even though 12 features' spec/plan docs were known-stale. The only warning was about uncommitted files, not about stamping over unreconciled drift.
issues.root_cause_guess: reconcile-stamp conflates 'bootstrap the anchor' with 'I finished reconciling'; it lacks a guard (or separate --bootstrap mode) that refuses or warns when known feature-doc drift exists at stamp time.

issues.title: dummyindex indexes its own installed .claude/ tooling, flooding reconcile with self-generated 'unassigned new files'
issues.evidence: L138 reconcile --json: "\"unassigned_new_files\": [\".claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/SKILL.md\", \".claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/agents/architecture.md\", ... \".claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/db-specialist-agent.md.tmpl\", ... \".claude/skills/dummyindex/council/65-reconcile.md\"]" — all 14 entries are dummyindex's own installer output. L123: "_New/unenriched code is a commit-anchored signal — it clears only when you reconcile."
issues.what_happened: Every file the 0.19.2 installer itself wrote into `.claude/skills/` showed up as drift the council is told to 'place' into the feature taxonomy. Claude verified no feature owns `.claude` paths (L141-142) and told the user the remaining noise "still settle[s] with a `reconcile-stamp --force` after you commit" — manual churn caused entirely by the tool's own artifacts.
issues.root_cause_guess: The file scanner has no default exclusion for `.claude/**` (or for paths the installer itself just wrote), so installer output is treated as project source needing curation.

issues.title: Installer ships inert equip template copies (.tmpl) into the repo skill folder that nothing reads — and will re-copy them after deletion
issues.evidence: User L191: "can i get rid of the .tmpl files ?" Claude L210: "The `equip` CLI renders specialists from the **installed package's** templates (`dummyindex/skills/equip/templates/`, resolved at `render.py:38`), never than the repo's `.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/` copy... a future `dummyindex install --scope project` will re-copy them (they're part of what the installer ships alongside the skill)" [verbatim except 'never from'].
issues.what_happened: `install --scope project` copied 5 specialist `*.md.tmpl` files into `.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/`. Claude proved via package source that neither the equip CLI nor SKILL.md ever reads the repo copies, deleted them at the user's request — but warned the next project-scope install will silently restore them, and the copies had also polluted the reconcile unassigned-files list.
issues.root_cause_guess: The installer blindly mirrors the whole skill package directory (including render-time templates the CLI resolves from site-packages) instead of shipping only agent-facing files.

issues.title: Upgrade leaves stale 0.15-era artifact `.context/_enrich_plan.json` (392KB) at the index root; no migration/cleanup
issues.evidence: L147: "-rwxr-xr-x 1 ahmed ahmed 392603 Jun  7 22:45 .context/_enrich_plan.json" (cache copy absent: "ls: cannot access '.context/cache/_enrich_plan.json': No such file or directory"). L183: "removed the stale 0.15-era `.context/_enrich_plan.json` (0.19 writes it under `cache/`)".
issues.what_happened: After rebuild/refresh under 0.19.2, the obsolete 0.15 enrich-plan file remained at the `.context/` root (0.19 relocated it under `cache/`). Claude had to discover this by listing mtimes and manually `rm` it (L154).
issues.root_cause_guess: rebuild/refresh only writes current-schema artifacts; there is no migration step or orphan-file sweep when the artifact layout changes between versions.

issues.title: preflight falsely reports 'not a git repo' for a directory that is on a git branch and later anchors to HEAD
issues.evidence:
L38 preflight: "## ⚠ Warnings
- not a git repo — writes won't be reversible via git.
...
- git: not a git repo" — yet event metadata shows gitBranch "development" at the same cwd, and L113 reconcile-stamp succeeds: "anchor advanced to a02e4370e939".
issues.what_happened: `dummyindex context preflight` on BOS-Mono/backend (a directory inside the BOS-Mono monorepo, likely a subdir or submodule rather than a repo root) warned that writes wouldn't be git-reversible, while the same tree was demonstrably on branch `development` and git anchoring worked minutes later. The false warning misinforms the safety disclosure preflight exists to provide. (Preflight ran under the old 0.15.0 binary; may persist in newer versions.)
issues.root_cause_guess: Git detection checks for a `.git` directory at the target root instead of `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`, failing for monorepo subdirectories / submodules (where `.git` is absent or a file).

issues.title: Known destructive behavior on record: full `rebuild` re-clusters into community-N and drops curated features
issues.evidence: L177 (project MEMORY.md surfaced in-session): "- [Full rebuild destroys curated features](full-rebuild-destroys-curated-features.md) — dummyindex full `rebuild` re-clusters into community-N, drops curated semantic features; use `--changed` or recover via git."
issues.what_happened: Not re-triggered in this session, but this prior-session lesson was loaded as project memory and actively shaped Claude's behavior (it exclusively used `--changed` and avoided ingest). It also makes reconcile's 're-run a fresh ingest' advice (L108) dangerous. Recorded here because the workaround burden persisted into this session.
issues.root_cause_guess: Plain `rebuild` defaults to destructive re-clustering with no confirmation or curated-content guard; safe incremental mode is opt-in via `--changed` instead of the default.

issues.title: features/INDEX.json field naming tripped a consumer: entries use `feature_id`, not `id`
issues.evidence:
L124: "Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 6, in <module>
    print(f['id'], ...)
          ~^^^^^^
KeyError: 'id'" — L130 shows the actual schema: "{ "feature_id": "workspace-org-core", "kind": "community", ... }".
issues.what_happened: Claude's first attempt to enumerate features from `.context/features/INDEX.json` failed with KeyError because it guessed `id`; the file uses `feature_id` and wraps entries under a `features` key. One retry was needed to learn the structure empirically.
issues.root_cause_guess: The JSON schema of `.context` artifacts isn't documented where agents look (HOW_TO_USE.md points at the file but not its field names), so consumers guess field names.

summary: Session (2026-06-10, BOS-Mono/backend): the user had upgraded dummyindex (0.15.0 → 0.19.2) and asked Claude to update .context/ and related wiring; a short follow-up asked whether installer-shipped .tmpl files could be deleted. Claude ultimately completed the upgrade (lock bump, skill refresh, hooks, incremental rebuild, anchor stamp) but most of the session was spent fighting dummyindex itself: a repo-venv copy shadowing the updated CLI, three mutually contradictory drift signals (rebuild vs plan-update vs reconcile), a destructive 'fresh ingest' migration suggestion, an anchor stamp that zeroed recorded drift without reconciling, self-generated .claude/ files polluting reconcile, and stale/inert artifacts (0.15 _enrich_plan.json, unused equip .tmpl copies) needing manual cleanup. No plugin discover/install was exercised in this session; hooks (SessionStart drift report, Stop nudge) fired correctly. The 12 stale features were left un-reconciled as an open item.

issues.title: New 0.24 Stop reconcile-gate immediately blocks session end, demanding a ~40+ agent council reconcile mostly for drift inherited from earlier sessions
issues.evidence: dummyindex reconcile gate: `.context/` is stale after a substantial session. Before this session ends, reconcile the drifted parts... for each drifted feature below, re-run its council enrichment (`/dummyindex --recouncil <feature>`)... Do NOT skip silently — this is the per-session reconcile gate. Drifted features: ai-chat-streaming, api-routers, app-infra-tools, artifacts-providers, assets-resources, auth-enums, docs-app-bootstrap, identity-invitations, integrations-oauth, llm-uploads, mcp-server, utils-config-fragment, workspace-org-core.
issues.what_happened: Right after Claude installed the new 0.24 hooks during a plain tool-update task, the first Stop fired a hook_blocking_error (L800) listing 13 drifted features + 26 unplaced files. Claude assessed the demanded reconcile as '~3-4 agents apiece = 40+ agent runs', noted several features 'likely drifted from earlier work, not this session', and had to interrupt with an AskUserQuestion; the user chose 'Defer to a dedicated session'. Block-once behavior did hold (next Stop at L833 passed), but the gate's all-or-nothing demand was wildly disproportionate to the session's actual ask (a version bump).
issues.root_cause_guess: reconcile-gate scopes its demand to total accumulated drift since the last stamp (commit-anchored), not to drift caused by the current session, and has no cheap partial/scoped-reconcile or defer verb surfaced in the block message despite a `hooks defer-check` verb existing.

issues.title: dummyindex indexes its own installed skill files as unplaced project source, polluting drift reports and the reconcile gate
issues.evidence: New unplaced files: .claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/db-specialist-agent.md.tmpl, .claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/docs-specialist-agent.md.tmpl, ... .claude/skills/dummyindex/council/22-parallel-dispatch.md, app/core/auth/service.py, ... — assistant: 'Several "new unplaced files" are dummyindex's **own** skill templates (`.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/*.tmpl`, `council/22-parallel-dispatch.md`) — those are tooling, not app code, and shouldn't be force-placed into features.'
issues.what_happened: Ten of dummyindex's own project-scope skill template files appeared as 'New files not yet in any feature' in three places: the SessionStart drift report (L4), the `rebuild --changed` output (L775, 'unassigned new files'), and the Stop reconcile-gate block (L800). Claude had to manually reason that force-placing the tool's own templates into the app's feature taxonomy would be wrong. Recurred 3x in this transcript.
issues.root_cause_guess: The new-file/ownership scanner has no exclusion for `.claude/` (or at least dummyindex-managed paths), so the tool's own equip/council assets are treated as application source needing feature placement.

issues.title: Four-layer version skew (CLI 0.23.0, skills 0.22.0, project dep 0.22, .context stamped 0.15.0) with no automatic detection; full manual sync required
issues.evidence: === uv-tool CLI (~/.local/bin) version === 0.23.0 ... === .context version stamp === "dummyindex_version": "0.15.0", "schema_version": 1 ... assistant: 'your CLI is now **0.23.0**..., but the `.claude` skills are still **0.22.0**, the project dev-dep is pinned **0.22.0**, and `.context/meta.json` was built way back on **0.15.0**.'
issues.what_happened: User asked 'i have updated dummyindex and i need you to update it locally and update .context and .claude accordingly'. Claude discovered every layer at a different version. Nothing in routine use had flagged this: the audit skill banner ('Installed from dummyindex 0.22.0. Run dummyindex --version to confirm the CLI matches') was never acted on earlier in the same session even though the CLI was already 0.23.0 then. The sync took ~10 probing/fixing tool calls plus two AskUserQuestion rounds and ended as PR #16.
issues.root_cause_guess: No version-coherence check at runtime (hooks/skills never compare CLI vs skill vs index stamp); the skill banner relies on Claude voluntarily running a check it has no trigger for.

issues.title: .context/meta.json version stamp can only move via a destructive --full rebuild, so it permanently lies after upgrades
issues.evidence: === rebuild --changed === ... context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster). ... === meta version after === "dummyindex_version": "0.15.0" ... assistant: '`meta.json` still stamps `dummyindex_version: 0.15.0`... `rebuild --changed` deliberately preserves it; bumping it to 0.24 would require a `--full` rebuild, which **re-clusters and destroys the curated features**. Not worth it.'
issues.what_happened: After upgrading the CLI to 0.24.0 and running the documented non-destructive refresh, meta.json (which was modified by the rebuild) still claims the index was built by 0.15.0. The only sanctioned way to update the stamp destroys curated taxonomy, so the user must accept a permanently false provenance stamp — defeating the stamp's purpose and any future tooling that keys off it.
issues.root_cause_guess: rebuild --changed rewrites meta.json counts but intentionally(?) skips dummyindex_version/schema_version; there is no migration/stamp-bump path decoupled from full re-cluster.

issues.title: Audit persona catalog hardcodes subagent_type names that don't exist in the equipped repo, forcing Claude to improvise agent mapping
issues.evidence: ---CATALOG ROLES--- architecture - Architecture & boundaries auditor -> Software Architect / correctness -> Code Reviewer / data-integrity -> Data Engineer / maintainability -> Code Reviewer / performance -> Performance Benchmarker / security -> Security Engineer / tests -> Test Results Analyzer ... assistant: '(Mapping catalog `subagent_type`s to this repo's available agents: architecture→Backend Architect, correctness/maintainability→backend-reviewer, security→Security Engineer, tests→Test Results Analyzer.)'
issues.what_happened: The repo's .claude/agents contains only backend-reviewer, db-manager, python-implementer, python-tester (equip-generated), but `audit start` emitted a catalog pointing at generic personas like 'Software Architect' and 'Performance Benchmarker'. Claude silently invented a mapping — including 'Backend Architect', which is also not in the repo's roster — so audit dispatch correctness depended on harness fallback rather than the catalog. The audit still completed, but the catalog's subagent_type field was effectively dead weight.
issues.root_cause_guess: audit catalog generation is static and not wired to equipment.json / the repo's actual agent roster; no resolution step maps personas to installed agents at scaffold time.

issues.title: CLI upgrade via uv tool landed in a snap-confined off-PATH bin, leaving the stale 0.23.0 binary active
issues.evidence: === upgrade to 0.24.0 via uv tool === ... Installed 1 executable: dummyindex warning: `/home/ahmed/snap/code/247/.local/share/../bin` is not on your PATH. To use installed tools, run `export PATH=...` or `uv tool update-shell`. === verify === 0.23.0 /home/ahmed/.local/bin/dummyindex
issues.what_happened: First upgrade attempt (`uv tool install dummyindex==0.24.0 --force`) succeeded but installed into a snap-rev-specific directory not on PATH; verification still showed the old 0.23.0 pip --user binary. Claude had to diagnose the real install method (pip --user console script under python3.11), redo the upgrade with `python3.11 -m pip install --user`, and later uninstall the stray uv-tool copy. The brand-new /dummyindex-update skill claims to detect install method but wasn't used (see separate issue).
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex has no canonical install/upgrade command that detects the existing install method; docs/skills assume uv tool, which breaks under snap-mangled XDG paths.

issues.title: /dummyindex-update skill unusable for the actual task because it can't pin a version (always resolves latest from GitHub)
issues.evidence: There's now a dedicated `/dummyindex-update` skill (new in 0.24) that does exactly this in one shot — but it resolves "latest from **GitHub**" and could move past the 0.24.0 you picked, so I'll finish manually at the pinned 0.24.0 to honor your choice.
issues.what_happened: The session was literally the update workflow the new skill exists for, yet Claude had to bypass it and hand-roll all five layers (CLI, skills, dep pin, hooks, .context refresh) because the skill offers no --version/pin argument and the user had explicitly chosen 0.24.0 over whatever GitHub-latest might be.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex-update skill design only supports 'latest tag or main'; no version parameter plumbed through to the reinstall steps.

issues.title: `context hooks install` reports 'skipped (already current)' while silently modifying .claude/settings.json (added the Stop reconcile-gate hook)
issues.evidence:
=== refresh SessionStart/Stop/PreCompact hooks === hooks install: skipped (already current): claude/SessionStart, claude/Stop, claude/PreCompact ... === what changed in .claude so far === M .claude/settings.json ... diff: + "command": "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH
command -v dummyindex >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0
dummyindex context reconcile-gate --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0
"
issues.what_happened: Immediately before the call, `git status --short .claude` showed settings.json clean (L758); after `hooks install`, settings.json was modified with a brand-new reconcile-gate Stop command — yet the CLI claimed all three hook events were 'skipped (already current)'. Claude only caught the change by diffing git, then had to explain the new hook to the user post-hoc.
issues.root_cause_guess: The 'already current' check is per hook-event (SessionStart/Stop/PreCompact exist) rather than per managed command, so adding a new command to an existing event isn't reported; output layer doesn't reflect actual writes.

issues.title: `rebuild --changed` says 'enriched index preserved' while simultaneously reporting removal of curated feature flow docs
issues.evidence: context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster). ... removed files: .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-057.json, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-057.md, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-058.json, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-058.md, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-061.json, .context/features/assets-resources/flows/flow-061.md
issues.what_happened: The non-destructive refresh printed contradictory output — 'enriched index preserved' next to a 'removed files' list naming six curated flows/* docs inside a feature directory (flows/*.md are explicitly listed by the drift report as curated docs to maintain). Claude had to run a follow-up git audit (grep for spec.md/plan.md/concerns.md/feature.json changes) to convince itself and the user nothing curated was clobbered; the flows ambiguity was never fully explained.
issues.root_cause_guess: rebuild prunes flow docs whose anchor symbols/files disappeared (the audit refactor split files), but reports it in the same breath as 'preserved' with no distinction between deterministic artefacts and council-enriched flow docs.

issues.title: Giant generated .context diffs (33k+/24k lines) make commits unreviewable and fed the user's branch-state confusion
issues.evidence: [development b5279b3] chore: update dummyindex to 0.24.0 + refresh .context backbone 10 files changed, 32841 insertions(+), 24391 deletions(-) ... assistant: 'One heads-up for whoever reviews #16: the diff is large by line count (~33k/24k) because the regenerated `.context/map/symbols.json` and `symbol-graph.json` are big generated JSON — it's deterministic output, not hand edits.'
issues.what_happened: A routine backbone refresh produced a 57k-line diff needing a reviewer disclaimer. Earlier in the session the user was confused ('i checkout to development are all the commits in all the branches and work trees on to dev?' / 'files looked reverted'): the local-vs-origin development divergence was 'almost entirely .context/ + lockfile' from scattered .context commits (chore/updated-context branch, PR #12 'Chore/updated context', a local-only feat(audit) commit). dummyindex's heavyweight generated JSON in git is a recurring source of branch noise and review pain.
issues.root_cause_guess: map/symbols.json and symbol-graph.json are monolithic regenerated files with unstable ordering/content; no diff-friendly sharding, .gitattributes guidance, or generated-file marking.

issues.title: Memory-nudge Stop hook flags a read-only audit session as needing a handoff; user pushes back
issues.evidence: hookAdditionalContext: 'dummyindex: this session is substantial (subagents: 15; ~179,628 main-thread output tokens) and no handoff has been saved this session. In one short line, offer the user the option to checkpoint a session handoff...' — next user message: 'there were no code changes'
issues.what_happened: After a purely read-only audit (findings written only under .context/audits/), the nudge fired on volume heuristics (subagent count + output tokens) and Claude offered /dummyindex-remember; the user's curt correction shows the offer felt off-target. Claude then agreed there was 'nothing to checkpoint' since the durable output was already on disk.
issues.root_cause_guess: Nudge heuristic considers only session size, not whether anything changed that a future session couldn't recover from disk; no suppression when the session's artifacts are already persisted.

issues.title: `audit show --json` returns dir: None, forcing shell hacks to locate the audit workspace
issues.evidence: slug: audit-the-backend-app-source-for-adherence-to-the-project-s dir: None mode: standard model: sonnet-4.6 max_rounds: 3 scope: ['app']
issues.what_happened: The conductor queried `dummyindex context audit show --slug ... --json` and the JSON payload's `dir` field was None even though the workspace existed; Claude had to resolve both the slug and the catalog path via `ls -t .context/audits | head -1` instead of the API. The rest of the audit flow (start, audit-log stamping across rounds 0-1, report synthesis) worked correctly.
issues.root_cause_guess: audit show omits or fails to compute the workspace directory in JSON mode (key mismatch or unpopulated field).

issues.title: `dummyindex context hooks --help` errors out instead of printing help
issues.evidence: === hooks verbs === error: usage: dummyindex context hooks install|uninstall|status|defer-check [--global]
issues.what_happened: While discovering the 0.24 surface, Claude ran `dummyindex context hooks --help` (and bare `dummyindex context hooks`) and got an error-styled usage line rather than help text, then had to grep the parent `context --help` output to learn the verbs. Minor, but it cost an extra probing round during an already long manual update.
issues.root_cause_guess: Hand-rolled subcommand parser treats --help / missing verb as an error path; no per-subcommand help.

summary: Session in BOS-Mono/backend (~2026-06-11/12): user asked whether code followed conventions, ran a /dummyindex-audit 5-persona argue-and-audit panel (which worked well — found 2 real critical bugs), had Claude fix all 18 findings into PR #13, untangled branch confusion, then asked Claude to sync an out-of-date dummyindex install ('update it locally and update .context and .claude accordingly'). The audit/council flow performed strongly, but the install/update/hooks surface struggled: four layers were at four different versions (CLI 0.23, skills 0.22, dep 0.22, .context stamped 0.15), the upgrade hit a snap/uv off-PATH trap, the new update skill couldn't pin a version, `hooks install` silently added the reconcile-gate while claiming 'skipped', and the freshly installed Stop reconcile-gate immediately blocked session end demanding a ~40+ agent reconcile that included dummyindex's own skill templates as 'unplaced files'. No equip/plugin discover-install activity occurred in this session, so plugin-wiring problems specifically were not exercised here.

issues.title: /dummyindex-plan run abandoned at blocking AskUserQuestion — no proposal artifact ever produced
issues.evidence: "The user doesn't want to proceed with this tool use. The tool use was rejected (eg. if it was a file edit, the new_string was NOT written to the file). STOP what you are doing and wait for the user to tell you how to proceed." ... "[Request interrupted by user for tool use]"
issues.what_happened: User invoked /dummyindex-plan to confirm/extend the MCP feature. Claude spent ~7 min (L16-L86) reading .context/ and source, ran an advisor call, then posed a 2-question, 6-option AskUserQuestion dialog (scope selection + which-org) at 11:39:53. The rejection event is timestamped 17:36:48 — the dialog sat unanswered for ~6 hours before the user dismissed it and interrupted. The session ends there: zero proposal files were written, `dummyindex context propose` was never run, and all grounding work was lost. Occurred once (it is the terminal event of the session). The slash command's allowedTools were only [Read, Write, Bash] (L13), so the dialog was the sole interactive gate and the only path forward.
issues.root_cause_guess: The plan flow front-loads long silent exploration and ends in a blocking multi-question modal before persisting anything durable. Nothing is scaffolded first, so an abandoned/rejected dialog discards the entire run. The skill should scaffold the proposal (cheap, deterministic) before any blocking user interaction, or degrade to free-text questions.

issues.title: Claude bypassed the skill's documented CLI path — only dummyindex command run was `--version`; grounding done by hand
issues.evidence: SKILL.md step 2: "Scaffold + ground (the CLI does this in one call): dummyindex context propose --slug <slug> --title \"<request>\" ... runs the deterministic consistency scan (reusing `query` — no LLM)" — yet the only dummyindex invocation in the session is: {"command": "echo \"=== dummyindex version ===\" && dummyindex --version 2>&1 | head -1; ..."}
issues.what_happened: The installed dummyindex-plan SKILL.md (v0.15.0) prescribes: resolve slug → `dummyindex context propose` (scaffold + deterministic consistency scan) → read the hits → flesh out prose. In the actual session Claude inverted this: it manually Read .context/HOW_TO_USE.md, two existing proposal specs, the feature spec, and six raw source files, ran an advisor, and went straight to user questions — never invoking `context propose` or `query`. The deterministic consistency scan the CLI is supposed to provide was re-derived by the model token-by-token, and because nothing was scaffolded, the interrupted session left no artifact. Single occurrence spanning the whole session.
issues.root_cause_guess: Skill prose ordering does not bind the model; the model judged 'confirm the build / extend an existing proposal' as not matching the scaffold-first flow (the skill documents no update/extend-existing-proposal path), so it improvised its own grounding workflow.

issues.title: Advisor cites skill provisions that the installed v0.15.0 skill does not contain (AskUserQuestion sanction)
issues.evidence: Advisor result (L75): "**Ask it as a single AskUserQuestion, behavior-framed (not token-vs-arg)** — the skill explicitly sanctions AskUserQuestion for choosing between approaches before finalizing." — the full installed SKILL.md text (4.7KB, v0.15.0, dumped in its entirety at L9) contains no mention of AskUserQuestion or any ask-the-user step.
issues.what_happened: The advisor steered Claude toward a blocking AskUserQuestion by asserting the skill 'explicitly sanctions' it; the v0.15.0 skill installed in this repo has no such provision (the user-question step appears only in later dummyindex-plan versions). Claude followed the advice — and also split it into 2 questions despite the advisor saying 'a single AskUserQuestion' and 'fold this into the same question' — leading directly to the dialog the user rejected (issue 1). One occurrence.
issues.root_cause_guess: Version skew between the latest dummyindex-plan skill semantics (which the advisor/model knows) and the older skill copy actually installed in the repo; nothing pins guidance to the installed skill version.

issues.title: Completed/superseded proposals linger in .context/proposals/ with no lifecycle, forcing the user to ask plan to 'confirm the build'
issues.evidence: Assistant (L27): "Substantial WIP MCP code already exists, plus an `mcp-server` feature in `.context/features/` and two proposals (`mcp-server`, `mcp-design-tokens-from-db`). Let me read what's already there before deciding what the proposal should be." User (L8): "i need you to confirm the mcp feature build, so far the spec and the plan was to port it from the old repo but now it has extended"
issues.what_happened: The mcp-server feature is already built and indexed under .context/features/mcp-server/, yet its originating proposal still sits live in .context/proposals/mcp-server/, alongside a second overlapping proposal (mcp-design-tokens-from-db) whose spec says it 'reverses MCP Decision 2'. The user had to ask the plan skill to 'confirm the mcp feature build', and Claude had to spend the whole session untangling which of three artifacts (built feature doc, stale port proposal, decision-reversal proposal) reflected reality before it could plan the extension. One occurrence, but it shaped the entire session.
issues.root_cause_guess: No proposal lifecycle in dummyindex (no archived/done/superseded status, no closure when build completes), and the plan skill documents only create-new (`--force` overwrite) — no extend/amend-existing-proposal verb.

issues.title: SessionStart hook labeled DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH only reports drift; one edited file fans out to 3 drift entries
issues.evidence:
"command": "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH
command -v dummyindex >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0
dummyindex context plan-update --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0
" → output: "4 source files across 4 features have been edited... - **auth-enums** — app/config.py - **integrations-oauth** — app/config.py - **utils-config-fragment** — app/config.py ... _Drift clears naturally: once you edit a feature doc, its mtime updates and these entries drop off._"
issues.what_happened: The hook ran successfully (exit 0, 1.9s) but three misleading details surfaced: (a) the marker comment says AUTO_REFRESH while the behavior is report-only; (b) the subcommand is named `context plan-update` yet produces a drift report, not an update; (c) a single edit to app/config.py raised drift against 3 separate features, and clearing relies on doc mtimes ('edit a feature doc' bumps mtime) rather than content reconciliation. The drift was never addressed during the session. One occurrence (session start).
issues.root_cause_guess: Legacy naming from when the hook was intended to auto-refresh, file-to-feature mapping at file granularity (shared config files pollute multiple features), and mtime-based drift accounting instead of the later commit-anchored model.

issues.title: As-built feature doc still marked `confidence: INFERRED` after the feature shipped through dummyindex's own pipeline
issues.evidence: L29, .context/features/mcp-server/spec.md: "# MCP Server — spec\n\n`confidence: INFERRED`"
issues.what_happened: The mcp-server feature was planned via a dummyindex proposal and built (the 'WIP: MCP port' commit), yet the index's own record of it carries the low-trust INFERRED confidence marker — the same marker used for council guesses about un-curated code. Claude consequently re-verified everything against raw source (auth.py, scoping.py, enums.py, services) instead of trusting the index, doubling the grounding work. One occurrence.
issues.root_cause_guess: At v0.15.0 there was no reconcile/mark-enriched promotion step closing the loop after a build, so docs for code built by dummyindex itself stay tagged as inferred and the index never gains authority over features it produced.

summary: Short (91-event) /dummyindex-plan session on the BOS-Mono backend (2026-06-09, dummyindex 0.15.0): the user asked to confirm and extend the already-built MCP feature plan. Claude grounded itself well (drift hook fired cleanly, .context/ navigation worked, advisor analysis was sharp) but never ran the documented `dummyindex context propose`/`query` path, and the run died when a blocking 2-question AskUserQuestion sat unanswered for ~6 hours and was rejected — no proposal artifact was ever produced. No CLI tracebacks or argparse failures occurred, and there is zero plugin discover/install/wire activity in this transcript because the repo's installed dummyindex (0.15.0) predates the plugin-manager feature; the struggles here are plan-flow UX, skill-version skew, and proposal-lifecycle/staleness issues rather than plugin wiring.

issues.title: User reports dummyindex-managed design plugins wired wrong — canvas-to-code/paper-design 'designing wrong pages'
issues.evidence: frontend and backend use dummyindex as sort of a package manager, it is responsible for managing all the plugins for those inner submodules... so for the frontnend you need to wire the paper-desigin and canvas-to-code plugin correctly through it, right now its not working as intended and desiging wrong pages
issues.what_happened: Opening user request. Despite dummyindex being the designated plugin manager, neither paper-design nor canvas-to-code was registered as equipment at all; plan/build had no way to route through them. The session found two latent causes dummyindex never surfaced: paper-design lived at the mono root so its .claude-design/ and .context/ grounding resolved against the wrong repo, and frontend/.canvas-to-code/config.yaml components_dirs still mapped to Phase-E-removed component tiers ('the mapper emits into a tree that no longer exists'). All fixes were manual.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex's plugin-manager layer tracks only its own equipment.json; it has no validation that an enabled plugin's run-root, grounding paths, or plugin-side config (.canvas-to-code/config.yaml) match the repo it is wired into, so misconfigured plugins silently produce wrong output.

issues.title: `equip --help` rejected, and bare `equip` executes a full apply — help probe mutated repo state
issues.evidence:
error: unknown argument(s) for `equip`: ['--help']
===INSTALL===
error: `equip install` requires <plugin>@<marketplace>
[next command, bare `dummyindex context equip`:]
equip: stack=typescript frameworks=['React', 'Next.js']
  write   typescript-implementer  ->  .claude/agents/typescript-implementer.md
...
equip: wrote 8 item(s), skipped 0 ... [git status:] M .context/equipment.json
issues.what_happened: Claude tried `equip --help` and `equip install --help` to learn the verbs; both errored. It then ran bare `dummyindex context equip` expecting a usage listing — instead the command performed a real apply, writing 8 files and dirtying .context/equipment.json (re-serialized capabilities arrays). Claude had to verify nothing semantic changed and flag the accident to the user in its final summary: 'One caveat to flag: while probing the equip CLI I ran a bare `dummyindex context equip` ... which re-serialized `equipment.json`'.
issues.root_cause_guess: Custom argparse-less CLI: no --help support on subcommands, and `equip` defaults to the destructive `apply` verb instead of printing usage when invoked with no arguments.

issues.title: `equip discover` and `equip install` are completely undocumented in CLI help
issues.evidence:
dummyindex context 2>&1 | grep -n "adopt\|discover\|install\|marketplace" →
8: --no-hooks skips installing
53: hooks install|uninstall|status|defer-check
285: equip uninstall [--root DIR] [--dry-run]
(no `equip discover` or `equip install` entries anywhere in help)
issues.what_happened: Claude grepped the full `dummyindex context` help for the plugin-manager verbs the skill advertises ('discover searches the marketplaces... install wires them natively') and found nothing — the help lists equip apply/add-specialist/status/refresh/reset/uninstall/patch only. It had to guess `equip discover`, `equip install`, and the flags --scope/--usage-doc/--repo/--yes by trial and error (3 failed/blocked attempts before success). Reproduced against the installed CLI 0.25.0 on this machine: discover/install absent from help.
issues.root_cause_guess: The discover/install verbs shipped in v0.19.0 were never added to the hand-maintained usage text in the context command's help string.

issues.title: `equip discover` returns irrelevant results with nonsense capability tags; ignores the query and misses the obvious plugin
issues.evidence:
equip discover (dry-run — nothing written):
  native figma@claude-plugins-official  covers: database
  native figma@knowledge-work-plugins  covers: database
  native 42crunch-api-security-testing@claude-plugins-official  covers: security, test
  native apollo-skills@claude-plugins-official  covers: performance
...
  native appwrite@claude-plugins-official  covers: -
issues.what_happened: Query was "design to code bridge". The output is an unranked dump of ~15 marketplace plugins with capability classifications that are plainly wrong (Figma 'covers: database'; appwrite covers '-'), nothing matching the query, and crucially it did NOT surface canvas-to-code even though that exact design-to-code plugin's marketplace was declared in the repo's own .claude/settings.json. Claude abandoned discover entirely and fell back to direct install.
issues.root_cause_guess: Discover's capability inference (likely keyword scoring over plugin metadata) mislabels plugins, the free-text query isn't actually used for filtering/ranking, and the candidate set only covers hardcoded official marketplaces — not marketplaces declared in the project's settings.json.

issues.title: `equip install` cannot resolve a marketplace already declared in the repo's own .claude/settings.json
issues.evidence:
error: canvas-to-code@canvas-to-code-marketplace not found in known marketplaces — if it lives in a low-profile repo, name it: --repo <owner>/<name>
EXIT:1
[yet frontend/.claude/settings.json already contained:]
"extraKnownMarketplaces": { "canvas-to-code-marketplace": { "source": { "source": "github", "repo": "opensesh/canvas-to-code" } } }
issues.what_happened: First install attempt failed even though the target settings.json — the very file install writes to — already mapped canvas-to-code-marketplace to opensesh/canvas-to-code. Claude had to re-run with an explicit --repo flag, duplicating information the tool could have read locally.
issues.root_cause_guess: Marketplace resolution consults only a built-in list of known marketplaces (claude-plugins-official etc.) and never reads extraKnownMarketplaces from the project/user settings.json it is about to modify.

issues.title: Trust gate blocks install of a plugin that is already enabled in committed team settings
issues.evidence:
error: canvas-to-code@canvas-to-code-marketplace requires approval (untrusted source). Re-run with --yes to approve.
EXIT:1
issues.what_happened: Second install attempt (with --repo) was blocked as 'untrusted' even though canvas-to-code@canvas-to-code-marketplace was already in enabledPlugins in the committed .claude/settings.json — i.e. the team had already approved it and the install was effectively a re-registration. Claude reasoned 'The plugin is already team-approved via committed settings' and passed --yes. Registering one already-installed plugin took 3 CLI invocations.
issues.root_cause_guess: The tiered-trust check classifies by marketplace origin only and doesn't treat existing enabledPlugins entries in the repo's settings.json as prior approval.

issues.title: equip install writes a near-empty manifest entry: kind 'agent' for a plugin, capabilities [] — useless for plan's `— via` routing
issues.evidence:
{
  "kind": "agent",
  "name": "canvas-to-code@canvas-to-code-marketplace",
  "path": ".claude/settings.json",
  "source": "marketplace",
  "capabilities": [],
  ...
  "version": null,
  "origin_hash": null,
  "mechanism": "native"
}
issues.what_happened: After a successful install, the equipment.json entry records the plugin as kind 'agent' (it is a plugin), with empty capabilities, null version/hash. Since /dummyindex-plan routes tasks via capabilities, the entry as written can never match a task. Claude immediately hand-edited equipment.json with a python script to set capabilities ['design','frontend']. There is also no CLI flag (e.g. --capabilities) to set them at install time, despite install accepting --usage-doc.
issues.root_cause_guess: Install only flips settings.json and appends a stub manifest record; it neither infers capabilities from the plugin's manifest/usage-doc nor exposes a flag to declare them, leaving the wiring half-done by design.

issues.title: No CLI verb to register local/in-repo skills as equipment — manifest hand-edited via python
issues.evidence:
python3 << 'EOF'
... if 'paper-design' not in names:
    d['items'].append(entry('paper-design', '.claude/skills/paper-design/SKILL.md', '.context/equipment/paper-design.md'))
if 'design-handoff' not in names:
    d['items'].append(entry('design-handoff', ...))
json.dump(d, open(p, 'w'), indent=2)
issues.what_happened: paper-design and design-handoff are local .claude/skills, not marketplace plugins, so `equip install <plugin>@<marketplace>` doesn't apply and no other verb registers them. To make plan's `— via` routing see them, Claude wrote raw JSON entries (source 'installed', mechanism 'skill') directly into .context/equipment.json — bypassing the tool's own schema guarantees (origin_hash null, no baseline).
issues.root_cause_guess: The equipment manifest supports source='installed' items but the CLI surface has no `equip adopt`/`equip register` verb for local tools; only marketplace installs and template-generated items have a sanctioned path.

issues.title: Garbage 'Frontend Developer' entry polluting backend equipment.json; no CLI verb to remove a single entry
issues.evidence:
{
  "kind": "agent",
  "name": "Frontend Developer",
  "path": "",
  "source": "installed",
  "capabilities": ["frontend"],
  "grounded_in": [],
  "subagent_type": "Frontend Developer",
  "version": null, "origin_hash": null, ... "mechanism": null
}
issues.what_happened: The backend's equipment.json contained an adopted agent entry with an empty path, no grounding, and no mechanism — Claude called it 'an odd "Frontend Developer" entry' and a frontend-capability agent has no business in the backend manifest anyway. Removal again required a hand-rolled python JSON edit; equip only offers reset NAME (re-render) and uninstall (everything pristine), not removal of one adopted entry.
issues.root_cause_guess: An earlier equip adopt/coverage-resolution pass recorded a registry agent by display name without validating path/grounding; combined with a missing `equip remove <name>` verb, broken adoptions persist until hand-edited.

issues.title: v0.25.0 `rebuild --changed` run from hooks 're-shatters the curated index' — auto-refresh hooks had to be reverted
issues.evidence:
4477cef6 revert(claude): drop auto rebuild --changed hooks — 0.25.0 re-shatters the curated index
29d029b3 chore(claude): sync dummyindex skill to 0.25.0
...
e5b91405 chore(claude): auto-keep .context current — LLM recouncil + deterministic refresh
issues.what_happened: The frontend submodule's recent git history (read during this session to decide where to commit) shows the team built auto-refresh hooks around `rebuild --changed`, then after upgrading to 0.25.0 had to revert them because the rebuild destroyed the curated taxonomy — directly contradicting the CLI help shown this same session: '--changed preserves the taxonomy + enrichment and only refreshes deterministic artefacts'. The user's original ask ('after each pass it should auto triggrer update for relevant .context') is exactly the capability this regression killed.
issues.root_cause_guess: Regression in 0.25.0's incremental rebuild: the curated/enriched detection fails (or hook execution context differs), so --changed falls through to the destructive re-cluster path despite documentation.

issues.title: equip's own generated prettier PostToolUse hook reformats equip's generated agents, breaking their pristine hash baselines
issues.evidence:
023d0731 chore(claude): keep prettier off generated .claude tooling so equip agents stay pristine
[frontend .prettierignore now contains:] 13:.claude
issues.what_happened: Frontend git history shows the team had to add .claude to .prettierignore because the DUMMYINDEX_EQUIP PostToolUse hook (npx prettier --write on every Write/Edit) was reformatting equip-generated agent/skill .md files, flipping them from pristine to user-modified — which per `equip refresh` semantics means 'USER_MODIFIED is skipped forever'.
issues.root_cause_guess: The generated formatter hook matches all Write|Edit paths including .claude/, and the origin-hash baseline is computed before the formatter runs, so equip's two generated artifacts fight each other unless the user manually excludes .claude from formatting.

issues.title: Commit-anchored reconcile requires repeated manual 'advance reconcile anchor' commits, even for equip's own output
issues.evidence:
4b212263 chore(context): advance reconcile anchor past equip specialist agents
a2b9795c chore(context): advance reconcile anchor to 4477cef6
75deddec chore(context): advance reconcile anchor to 94945054
issues.what_happened: Three of the ten most recent frontend commits are pure anchor-advancement bookkeeping, one explicitly to move the anchor 'past equip specialist agents' — i.e. files dummyindex itself generated tripped its own drift gate and required a manual stamp commit. Recurs 3x in the visible log window.
issues.root_cause_guess: The Model-B reconcile anchor counts every commit as potential drift, including dummyindex-generated .claude/ tooling and config-only changes; there is no exclusion for the tool's own outputs, so users pay a stamp-commit tax per equip run.

issues.title: CLI help says equipment.json is '(v2)' but the file it writes is schema_version 3
issues.evidence:
equip [apply] ... Build loop — render this repo's project-tuned toolkit into .claude/ from .context/ + preflight and record it in .context/equipment.json (v2)
[actual file:] { "schema_version": 3, "items": [ ...
issues.what_happened: The help text Claude relied on while reverse-engineering the equip surface states the manifest is v2; the frontend's actual equipment.json (and the entries install writes) are schema_version 3. Minor, but it compounds the documentation-vs-behavior mismatches that forced trial-and-error throughout this session.
issues.root_cause_guess: Help string not updated when the manifest schema was bumped to v3 for the plugin-manager fields (marketplace/origin_repo/mechanism).

summary: Session in the BOS-Mono monorepo (mono root + frontend/backend git submodules): the user asked Claude to reorganize three .claude configs and wire paper-design + canvas-to-code through dummyindex as the submodules' plugin manager, explicitly because the current wiring was 'not working as intended and desiging wrong pages'. dummyindex's context/equip basics (status, version, generated agents) worked, but the plugin-manager path was rough end to end: discover/install are undocumented in CLI help, a help probe ran a destructive bare `equip` apply, discover returned irrelevant results with nonsense capability tags and missed the obvious plugin, install failed to read the repo's own marketplace declarations and trust state, and the resulting manifest entry was so empty that Claude hand-edited equipment.json with python for all the real wiring (capabilities, local skills, removing a garbage entry). Git history read during the session also documents a 0.25.0 regression where `rebuild --changed` 're-shatters the curated index', plus equip's prettier hook breaking its own pristine baselines and recurring manual reconcile-anchor commits. Claude ultimately completed the reorg manually and committed locally in all three repos as asked.

issues.title: Plugin wired with dangling grounded_in: equipment.json referenced a playbook that never existed
issues.evidence:
"=== canvas-to-code.md in git history? ===
(empty = never existed)
=== grep refs to canvas-to-code.md ===
.context/equipment.json
.context/playbooks/design-workflow.md
.context/playbooks/impeccable.md" ... assistant: "The canvas-to-code playbook is referenced by `equipment.json`, `design-workflow.md`, and `impeccable.md` — but doesn't exist in the playbooks dir. That's the key gap for part 2."
issues.what_happened: The user asked to "make sure the new plugins (canvas-to-code and impeccable design) work through dummyindex". Investigation showed the canvas-to-code plugin entry in .context/equipment.json had "grounded_in": [".context/playbooks/canvas-to-code.md"] — but git history proved that file NEVER existed. Two other playbooks (design-workflow.md, impeccable.md) also referenced it. Claude had to hand-author the entire playbook from scratch (reading the plugin cache, .canvas-to-code/config.yaml, gates, tier system) to repair the wiring. Three files carried the dangling reference.
issues.root_cause_guess: A previous equip/plugin-install run recorded a grounding artifact path in equipment.json (and cross-referenced it from sibling playbooks) without ever generating the playbook file — install writes the manifest pointer but has no step that creates or verifies the grounding doc exists.

issues.title: Drift report is ~46% noise: dummyindex's own generated tooling re-flagged as unassigned after rebase despite prior force-stamp
issues.evidence: Drift report: "119 file(s) added since the index was last reconciled are owned by no feature... - `.claude/CLAUDE.md` - `.claude/agents/frontend-reviewer.md` ... - `.claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/SKILL.md`" ... triage: "=> left unowned: 55 Counter({'.claude (dummyindex tooling)': 45, '.claude-design (design inputs)': 7, 'docs/spikes': 3})" ... prior session memory: "50 `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` files are intentionally unowned — anchor force-stamped past them."
issues.what_happened: After the user rebased, the SessionStart drift hook dumped all 119 "unassigned new files" into session context — 55 of them (46%) were dummyindex's own installed skills/agents/CLAUDE.md, design inputs, and spike docs that the PREVIOUS session had already triaged as intentionally unowned and force-stamped past. The rebase reset the commit-anchored diff and brought them all back, forcing Claude to re-classify the same 55 paths again. Recurrence: second session in a row doing this triage.
issues.root_cause_guess: Commit-anchored reconcile has no persistent ignore/exempt list — "intentionally unowned" decisions live only in the anchor position, so any history rewrite (rebase) or anchor regression resurrects them. dummyindex should at minimum auto-exclude its own managed paths (.claude/skills/dummyindex*, generated agents, CLAUDE.md) from unassigned-file reporting.

issues.title: Second plugin (impeccable) wired with empty grounded_in despite its playbook existing
issues.evidence: equipment.json: "name": "impeccable@impeccable", ... "grounded_in": [], "subagent_type": null ... assistant: "Now let me fix the impeccable equipment entry — its `grounded_in` is empty `[]`, but `.context/playbooks/impeccable.md` exists. I'll wire it so impeccable is grounded the same way canvas-to-code is."
issues.what_happened: The impeccable plugin's equipment.json entry had an empty grounded_in array even though .context/playbooks/impeccable.md existed on disk. Claude manually edited equipment.json to add the playbook references. Both installed plugins thus had broken grounding (one dangling, one empty) — i.e. neither was actually "working through dummyindex" as the manifest implied.
issues.root_cause_guess: The install/wire step doesn't link existing playbooks into grounded_in — grounding appears to be populated inconsistently (or only at generation time for generated specialists, never back-filled for marketplace plugins).

issues.title: Plugin capability detection miscategorized a design-polish plugin as 'security'
issues.evidence:
equipment.json impeccable entry: "capabilities": [
  "security",
  "frontend"
] — while the repo's own playbook describes it as: "improve / critique frontend design (impeccable)... more polished, more consistent, more accessible, bolder or quieter"
issues.what_happened: The impeccable plugin (Paul Bakaus's design critique/polish plugin, ~23 design commands) was recorded in equipment.json with capabilities ["security","frontend"]. "security" is wrong — the plugin does design improvement/accessibility. Since equip's gap detection and plan's '— via <tool>' task annotation route work by capability, this mislabel can route security tasks to a design plugin and leave the real design-review capability gap undetected.
issues.root_cause_guess: Capability inference during plugin install (likely keyword-matching on plugin description/commands — e.g. 'audit'/'review' terms) maps design-critique language onto the 'security' capability with no human-readable validation step.

issues.title: No CLI verb to repair plugin grounding — Claude hand-edits the managed equipment.json manifest
issues.evidence: Edit tool on /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/frontend/.context/equipment.json: old_string "\"grounded_in\": []," -> new_string "\"grounded_in\": [\".context/playbooks/impeccable.md\", \".context/playbooks/design-workflow.md\"]" (and a second Edit adding design-workflow.md to the canvas-to-code entry)
issues.what_happened: To fix the two broken grounded_in entries, Claude edited .context/equipment.json directly with the Edit tool — twice. equipment.json is a dummyindex-managed, schema-versioned (v3), hash-baselined artifact; there is no documented `equip` verb for wiring/grounding repairs, so the only path was improvised raw-JSON surgery on the manifest the lifecycle (status/refresh/reset) depends on.
issues.root_cause_guess: The equip CLI exposes generation/lifecycle verbs but no `wire`/`ground`/`set-grounding` mutation verb for plugin entries, forcing manual edits that the documented "sanctioned patch seam" doesn't cover for the manifest itself.

issues.title: assign-files takes only repeated --file flags — bulk assignment of 64 files required an eval/repr shell hack
issues.evidence: Command: "args=$(python3 -c \"print(' '.join('--file ' + repr(l) for l in open('/tmp/bh_files.txt').read().splitlines()))\")\neval \"dummyindex context assign-files --feature brand-hub $args\"" — help text: "assign-files [--root DIR] --feature ID --file PATH [--file PATH]..."
issues.what_happened: To assign the 64 genuinely-new brand-hub files, Claude first had to write the list to /tmp/bh_files.txt, then build a 64-flag command line via python repr() and shell eval. It worked ("brand-hub now owns 154 file(s), 316 member(s)") but the documented interface has no --from-file/stdin mode, so the standard reconcile flow for any non-trivial delta requires fragile shell quoting gymnastics (paths here contain parentheses like 'app/(dashboard)/...').
issues.root_cause_guess: assign-files/unassign-files/scaffold-feature were designed for a handful of files; reconcile deltas routinely produce dozens, and the CLI lacks a file-list or stdin input mode.

issues.title: Committed equipment.json points at gitignored settings.local.json as the wiring location for impeccable
issues.evidence: equipment.json impeccable entry: "path": ".claude/settings.local.json", "source": "marketplace", ... "mechanism": "native" — vs canvas-to-code entry: "path": ".claude/settings.json". settings.local.json: "enabledPlugins": { "impeccable@impeccable": true }
issues.what_happened: The two plugins were wired at different scopes: canvas-to-code team-wide in .claude/settings.json, impeccable only in .claude/settings.local.json (local, typically gitignored). The committed .context/equipment.json manifest records impeccable as equipped with its path pointing at the local-only file, so any teammate cloning the repo gets a manifest claiming a plugin is wired that is not actually enabled for them.
issues.root_cause_guess: Install lets the wiring land in settings.local.json (possibly trust-tier default) but the equipment manifest doesn't distinguish committed vs local scope, creating a manifest/reality mismatch for other clones.

issues.title: Plugins shoehorned into schema as kind:'agent' with null version/origin_hash — lifecycle can't hash-verify plugin wiring
issues.evidence: equipment.json: { "kind": "agent", "name": "canvas-to-code@canvas-to-code-marketplace", ... "subagent_type": "canvas-to-code:canvas-to-code-pm", "version": null, "origin_hash": null, ... } and { "kind": "agent", "name": "impeccable@impeccable", ... "subagent_type": null, "version": null, "origin_hash": null }
issues.what_happened: Both marketplace plugins are recorded with kind "agent" although they are plugins (impeccable is "the `impeccable:impeccable` skill + ~23 /impecca... commands" per its own playbook, with subagent_type null). Both also carry version:null and origin_hash:null, so the hash-baselined lifecycle (status/refresh/reset "never clobbering a user edit") has nothing to baseline plugin entries against — manual edits or upstream changes are invisible to it.
issues.root_cause_guess: Schema v3 has no 'plugin' kind and the installer doesn't compute a baseline hash or record a resolved version for marketplace items, only origin_ref.

issues.title: `rebuild --changed` (billed as incremental) ran full AST extraction over all 1086 files
issues.evidence: Help: "rebuild [--changed] [--full] ... (use --changed for incremental)". Output: "AST extraction: 100/1086 files (9%) ... AST extraction: 1086/1086 files (100%)\ncontext rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster)."
issues.what_happened: Claude ran `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` after the rebase; despite the flag being documented as incremental, the tool re-extracted AST for every one of the 1086 files. It completed and preserved the enriched index, but "incremental" only describes taxonomy preservation, not work avoided — misleading naming and unnecessary cost on large repos.
issues.root_cause_guess: --changed gates re-clustering/enrichment-preservation but the deterministic extraction layer has no per-file change detection (or ignores the cache manifest), so it always does a full pass.

issues.title: Known cosmetic index inaccuracy carried across sessions: INDEX confidence column reads EXTRACTED while docs are INFERRED
issues.evidence: SessionStart memory (now.md, injected into this session): "INDEX `confidence` column reads EXTRACTED (cosmetic; spec/plan carry INFERRED)."
issues.what_happened: The session-memory handoff injected at SessionStart explicitly flags that the INDEX.md confidence column shows EXTRACTED even though the feature spec.md/plan.md carry `confidence: INFERRED` (both brand-hub docs read in this session indeed show "`confidence: INFERRED`").
