The mismatch survived the previous session's full build and this session's rebuild --changed — nothing fixed it.
issues.root_cause_guess: INDEX.md regeneration derives confidence from feature.json's deterministic field instead of the enriched spec/plan confidence markers, and no reconcile/rebuild step syncs them.

issues.title: dev-pick recommends a generic persona ('Frontend Developer') instead of the repo's equipped specialist agents; dispatch result never seen
issues.evidence: dev-pick output: {"persona_id": "dev-frontend", "subagent_type": "Frontend Developer", "framework": "React", "fallbacks": ["Senior Developer", "general-purpose"]} — followed by Agent dispatch: {"description": "Re-enrich brand-hub .context docs", "subagent_type": "Frontend Developer", ...} — the transcript ends immediately after, with no tool result.
issues.what_happened: For brand-hub re-enrichment, `dummyindex context dev-pick` returned subagent_type "Frontend Developer" — not any of the repo's equip-generated agents (typescript-implementer / typescript-tester / frontend-reviewer) registered in equipment.json. Claude dispatched the Agent with that type and the transcript ends right there with no result, no mark-enriched, and no reconcile-stamp — the reconcile loop's completion is unverified and brand-hub's .pending-enrichment marker (which blocks reconcile-stamp) was last seen still set.
issues.root_cause_guess: dev-pick draws from a static council persona catalog disconnected from equipment.json, so its primary recommendation may not correspond to any installed subagent type; if 'Frontend Developer' isn't a registered agent the dispatch would fail outright (possibly why the session ends here, though truncation could also explain it).

issues.title: Reconcile JSON too large for casual inspection — first read truncated mid-JSON, command re-run
issues.evidence: First call: "dummyindex context reconcile --json 2>&1 | head -100" returned JSON cut off inside unassigned_new_files; Claude immediately re-ran: "dummyindex context reconcile --json 2>&1 | python3 -c \"import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('has_drift:', d['has_drift']); ... print('total unassigned:', len(d['unassigned_new_files']))\"" — "total unassigned: 119"
issues.what_happened: The reconcile --json payload enumerates every unassigned file inline (119 paths here), so `head -100` produced unparseable truncated JSON and the read-only reconcile had to be executed twice more (once for the summary, once at L99 to re-derive the file split). Minor, but every consumer of the report pays for the full path dump; there is no --summary or count-only mode.
issues.root_cause_guess: reconcile --json has no compact/summary output mode, and the human-readable drift report (SessionStart hook) likewise prints the complete file list rather than bucketed counts with a cap.

summary: Short post-rebase maintenance session (2026-06-11, BOS-Mono frontend submodule, 157 events): the user asked Claude to (1) update .context/ after a rebase and (2) "make sure the new plugins (canvas-to-code and impeccable design) work through dummyindex" — the request itself implying prior equip/install wiring hadn't finished the job. The dummyindex CLI never errored (reconcile, rebuild --changed, assign-files, dev-pick all succeeded), but the plugin wiring was demonstrably defective: canvas-to-code's equipment.json grounding pointed at a playbook that never existed in git history, impeccable's grounding was empty and its capabilities miscategorized as "security", and Claude had to hand-author the missing playbook and raw-edit the managed manifest to repair things. The other big toil source was the drift report: 55 of 119 "unassigned" files (46%) were dummyindex's own tooling and design inputs that a prior session had already triaged and force-stamped past, resurrected by the rebase. The transcript cuts off mid-task at the brand-hub re-enrichment Agent dispatch (subagent_type "Frontend Developer", not an equipped agent), so mark-enriched/reconcile-stamp completion is never observed.

issues.title: equip discover --repo obra/superpowers never surfaces the explicitly requested repo
issues.evidence:
$ dummyindex context equip discover "superpowers" --repo obra/superpowers
equip discover (dry-run — nothing written):
  native ai-literacy-superpowers@ai-literacy-superpowers  covers: review
  native autopilot@autopilot  covers: -
  native maxoreric-superpowers@maxoreric-marketplace  covers: -
  native superpowers@claude-plugins-official  covers: database, security, frontend, test, review
  ...
Install one with: equip install <plugin>@<marketplace> [--yes]
A low-profile repo `gh search` misses: add --repo <owner>/<name>.
issues.what_happened: The user invoked /dummyindex-equip with the explicit URL https://github.com/obra/superpowers. Claude ran discover twice (plain query, then with --repo obra/superpowers). Neither output contained any obra/superpowers candidate — the --repo run returned the same generic marketplace list, and the footer still advises 'add --repo <owner>/<name>' even though --repo WAS used. The user's exact target was never classified, so Claude could not install what was actually asked for; the user was steered to a different source (superpowers@claude-plugins-official).
issues.root_cause_guess: The --repo pinning path in equip discover silently fails to resolve/classify a direct GitHub repo (possibly gh search/API miss, or the flag merges the pinned repo into ranked marketplace results and drops it instead of force-including it). No error or 'repo not resolved' message is emitted, so the failure is invisible.

issues.title: Discover 'covers' capability metadata is wrong and inconsistent across copies of the same plugin
issues.evidence:
native superpowers@claude-plugins-official  covers: database, security, frontend, test, review
  native superpowers@superpowers-copilot  covers: database, review
  native superpowers@superpowers-dev  covers: -
  native autopilot@autopilot  covers: -
issues.what_happened: The same superpowers plugin family got three different capability sets depending on which marketplace re-publishes it (5 caps vs 2 vs none). superpowers is a process-discipline plugin (brainstorming, TDD, debugging, plan-writing) — 'database' and 'frontend' coverage is plainly wrong. A plugin named 'autopilot' with covers: '-' also ranked for the query 'superpowers'. These bogus capabilities then flowed unchallenged into the equipment.json manifest (see separate issue), where downstream coverage resolution and plan's '— via <tool>' annotation will consume them.
issues.root_cause_guess: Capability extraction appears keyword/heuristic-based on marketplace listing text rather than on actual plugin contents, so per-marketplace listing wording produces divergent and inflated 'covers' sets; query matching also admits weak name matches.

issues.title: Discover results are non-deterministic between consecutive runs
issues.evidence: Run 1 candidates: ai-literacy-superpowers, autopilot, bg-superpowers@bg-superpowers-marketplace, superpowers@claude-plugins-official, superpowers-devops. Run 2 (~1 min later, same query + --repo): ai-literacy-superpowers, autopilot, maxoreric-superpowers@maxoreric-marketplace, superpowers@claude-plugins-official, superpowers@superpowers-copilot, superpowers@superpowers-dev, superpowers-devops
issues.what_happened: Two discover invocations for the identical query 'superpowers' about a minute apart returned different candidate sets (bg-superpowers disappeared; maxoreric-superpowers, superpowers-copilot, superpowers-dev appeared). The churn makes plugin selection unreproducible and made it impossible to tell whether --repo changed anything or the search just returned a different page.
issues.root_cause_guess: Discover relies on live `gh search` (known secondary-rate-limit/ranking flakiness per the project's own notes) without caching, deduping, or a stable sort, so each call samples a different slice of results.

issues.title: Manifest records marketplace plugin as kind 'agent' with null version/origin_hash; status shows '(v-)'
issues.evidence: { "kind": "agent", "name": "superpowers@claude-plugins-official", "path": ".claude/settings.json", "source": "marketplace", "capabilities": ["database","security","frontend","test","review"], ... "version": null, "origin_hash": null, ... "origin_ref": null, "mechanism": "native" }  /  equip status: pristine superpowers@claude-plugins-official  (v-)
issues.what_happened: After install, the .context/equipment.json entry for the plugin has kind 'agent' (it is a plugin, not an agent), carries the bogus discover capabilities verbatim, and has null version, origin_hash, and origin_ref — equip status renders it '(v-)'. The hash-baselined lifecycle (status/refresh/reset detecting dirty vs pristine) has no baseline for native marketplace installs, so 'pristine' is asserted without anything to compare against and future upstream plugin changes are undetectable.
issues.root_cause_guess: The manifest schema was designed around generated file-based tools (agents/skills with hashable file contents); native settings.json plugin installs were bolted on without a plugin-specific kind or a mechanism to pin a marketplace version/commit, leaving baseline fields null.

issues.title: No duplicate-scope detection: plugin already active at user scope, equip discover/install gave no warning
issues.evidence: 1. **superpowers is already active in this session** — its skills (`brainstorming`, `debugging`, `test-driven-development`, etc.) are loaded, almost certainly from a **user-scope** install. Equipping it again at project scope would duplicate it.
issues.what_happened: superpowers was already enabled at user scope (its SessionStart hook had injected content into this very session). Neither equip discover nor equip install nor equip status surfaced this; Claude had to infer the duplication from session context on its own and warn the user manually. The install proceeded, producing dual-scope enablement (user + project) that the CLI never acknowledged.
issues.root_cause_guess: equip only inspects the project's .claude/settings.json and its own equipment.json; it never reads ~/.claude/settings.json (or the live plugin registry) to detect already-enabled plugins at other scopes.

issues.title: User's explicit source (obra/superpowers) silently substituted based on an unverified equivalence claim
issues.evidence: **There's a trusted official packaging.** `superpowers@claude-plugins-official` is **inert + trusted** ... Forcing it from **obra/superpowers** directly comes through as **untrusted** and would require `--yes`. Both are the same plugin family (obra's superpowers); the official marketplace just re-publishes it.
issues.what_happened: Because discover never resolved obra/superpowers (see high-severity issue), Claude asserted — with no tool output backing it — that the official marketplace entry 'is the same plugin family' and that the obra repo 'comes through as untrusted', then framed the AskUserQuestion around that claim. The user consented, but the comparison was never actually performed by the CLI; the repo the user typed in the slash-command args was never installed.
issues.root_cause_guess: The equip skill's trust-tiering guidance pushes hard toward trusted marketplace entries, and with discover failing on the pinned repo there was no CLI-provided fact to compare sources, leaving Claude to improvise an equivalence claim.

issues.title: First /dummyindex-equip invocation (no args) abandoned and retried with args 27s later
issues.evidence: L27 16:20:29 <command-name>/dummyindex-equip</command-name> (no args, no assistant response follows) → L31 16:20:56 <command-name>/dummyindex-equip</command-name> <command-args>https://github.com/obra/superpowers</command-args>
issues.what_happened: The user invoked /dummyindex-equip with no arguments, got no response (interrupted before the assistant replied), and re-invoked 27 seconds later with the GitHub URL. Weak signal, but suggests the no-arg entry point did not immediately make clear how to target a specific plugin, prompting a cancel-and-retype.
issues.root_cause_guess: Likely simple user retry after forgetting the argument; possibly the skill's no-arg flow (full equip pass) was not what the user wanted for a targeted plugin install, and the skill doc doesn't advertise the URL-arg form prominently.

summary: Short session (93 events) in the BOS-Mono backend submodule: the user checked the dummyindex version (0.22.0, skill and CLI matched), then ran /dummyindex-equip with the explicit URL https://github.com/obra/superpowers. The equip flow mechanically succeeded end-to-end — discover, AskUserQuestion source/scope choices, usage-interview playbook written to .context/equipment/superpowers.md, native install wired into .claude/settings.json, manifest recorded — and the user expressed no frustration beyond an 'is it all done?' check. However, dummyindex visibly struggled with the SPECIFIC plugin requested: `equip discover --repo obra/superpowers` never surfaced the obra repo at all (failing silently), discover's capability metadata was wrong and inconsistent across marketplaces, results were non-deterministic between runs, the manifest recorded the plugin as kind 'agent' with null version/hash (no drift baseline, status '(v-)'), and the CLI never detected that superpowers was already enabled at user scope — so the user ended up installing a substitute source (claude-plugins-official) based on an equivalence claim the tooling never verified.

issues.title: Equipment matcher routes a pure DB/schema GATE task to python-implementer
issues.evidence:
"text": "**GATE** — Mechanism = Supabase CLI migrations (`supabase/migrations/*.sql`, `supabase db push`; DECISIONS §15), applied/tested via the Supabase MCP on a `create_branch` branch... Decide the RLS/tenant-isolation model... Blocks all DDL."
  },
  "agent": "python-implementer",
  "subagent_type": "python-implementer",
  "fallback": false,
  "equipped": true
issues.what_happened: `dummyindex context build --proposal brand-centric-migration --next --json` returned the checklist's first item — a database-migration gate (Supabase CLI migrations, baseline migration, RLS/tenant-isolation decision) — and matched it to the generic `python-implementer` agent with `fallback: false` (i.e. the matcher considered this a confident, equipped match). The prior session's recap shows the repo had DB-focused agents (an adopted 'Data Engineer' pointer and a user-owned `db-manager` agent), yet none of them were selected for an item that is entirely about schema/DDL/Supabase work. The user interrupted the session immediately after this result was printed, before any dispatch happened. Occurred once (session ended right after).
issues.root_cause_guess: In v0.15.x the checklist→equipment matching is keyword/heuristic-based against equipment.json and likely defaults to the stack implementer for anything code-adjacent; user-owned/adopted specialist agents (db-manager, Data Engineer) either weren't registered in equipment.json or the matcher's coverage resolution doesn't consider capability domains (db vs code), so a DB gate falls through to the stack implementer. (Specialist generation + resolve_coverage precedence only landed in v0.17.0.)

issues.title: Build loop offers a human-decision GATE (+ main-session-only MCP grounding) as a dispatchable subagent item
issues.evidence: Skill: "Dispatch via `subagent_type` through the Task tool. Launch the Task tool with `subagent_type` set to the value the CLI emitted... that is the correct fallback, dispatch it as-is." CLI item: "Decide the RLS/tenant-isolation model + the two `DECISIONS.md §12` open questions (default-brand deletion; project-member read-vs-manage). Blocks all DDL." Next event: "[Request interrupted by user]"
issues.what_happened: The first checklist item is a decision gate: it requires settling the RLS/tenant-isolation model and two open design questions (things only the user can decide) and grounding against the live DB via the claude.ai-hosted Supabase MCP (`list_tables`/`create_branch`/`apply_migration`), tools a Task subagent typically can't reach. The skill's literal loop says to dispatch whatever `--next` emits via the Task tool 'as-is'. The user interrupted the session the moment this item+agent pairing was printed, and the build never progressed past 0/27.
issues.root_cause_guess: Neither the plan/checklist generator nor the build skill distinguishes 'decision/gate items needing user input or main-session MCP tools' from 'implementable code items'; the skill's loop has no branch for items that must be handled in the main session or escalated to the user instead of dispatched to a subagent.

issues.title: Installed per-repo skill (0.15.0) diverges from CLI (0.15.3); divergence noticed but reinstall instruction not followed
issues.evidence: Skill header: "> **Installed from dummyindex `0.15.0`.** Run `dummyindex --version` to confirm the CLI matches. If they diverge, re-run `dummyindex install --scope user`." ... Bash result of `dummyindex --version && dummyindex context build --proposal brand-centric-migration --status`: "0.15.3\nbuild status [brand-centric-migration]: 0/27 done"
issues.what_happened: The repo's `.claude/skills/dummyindex-build/SKILL.md` was rendered by dummyindex 0.15.0 while the CLI on PATH was 0.15.3. Claude ran the version check as instructed, saw the divergence, and silently proceeded with the stale skill instead of re-running `dummyindex install --scope user`. The stale skill text also describes the old strictly-serial 'flat checklist top-to-bottom' loop (the repo skill_listing shows the same outdated description), so the session was driven by superseded instructions. Seen on both invocations of /dummyindex-build in this session.
issues.root_cause_guess: CLI upgrades (uv tool) don't refresh per-repo/user skill copies, and the only safeguard is a prose instruction the agent can rationalize away (patch-level mismatch looks harmless). No machine check: the CLI itself could detect skill-version < CLI-version and warn or auto-refresh on first invocation.

issues.title: Prior session: equip's generated/adopted specialists needed manual hand-trimming and a hand-written db agent; user asked to improve equip itself
issues.evidence: away_summary: "Goal was equipping this backend repo with dummyindex tooling. I refreshed the four generated agents, adopted then hand-trimmed a Data Engineer pointer, and wrote a user-owned db-manager agent. Last I gave you a prompt to improve dummyindex's equip itself. Next: tell me how to proceed."
issues.what_happened: The session-resume recap recorded in this transcript summarizes the immediately preceding session in this repo: equip's output was not usable as-rendered — an adopted 'Data Engineer' registry pointer had to be hand-trimmed, the database capability gap had to be filled with a manually written user-owned `db-manager` agent (equip produced nothing adequate for it), and the work ended with the user requesting a prompt to improve dummyindex's equip feature itself. This is corroborated in-session by issue #1: even after that manual work, the build matcher still didn't use the DB agents.
issues.root_cause_guess: At v0.15.x equip only rendered the four stack agents (implementer/tester/reviewer/verify) and shallow registry pointers; no capability-specialist generation existed, so db coverage required manual authoring — and manually authored agents weren't integrated into the equipment manifest the build matcher reads.

issues.title: SessionStart drift report miscounts files and triples drift noise for a single edited file
issues.evidence:
"## .context/ drift report

4 source files across 4 features have been edited since the matching `.context/features/<id>/` docs were last touched...
- **app-infra-tools** — app/main.py
- **auth-enums** — app/config.py
- **integrations-oauth** — app/config.py
- **utils-config-fragment** — app/config.py"
issues.what_happened: The SessionStart hook (`# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH` running `dummyindex context plan-update`) reported '4 source files across 4 features' when only 2 distinct files changed (app/main.py and app/config.py); app/config.py alone produced three separate feature drift entries (one of them a 'utils-config-fragment' fragment-feature). The hook also swallows all errors (`2>/dev/null || true`), so any failure would be invisible. Appeared once at session start; the report was then ignored for the rest of the session.
issues.root_cause_guess: Drift counting iterates (feature, file) pairs instead of deduplicating files, and the feature taxonomy maps one shared file (config.py) into multiple fine-grained features, amplifying drift noise; mtime-based detection plus per-pair listing makes a one-line config edit look like 4-file drift.

issues.title: First /dummyindex-build invocation aborted by advisor model API error (environmental, not dummyindex)
issues.evidence: API Error: 400 tools.11.model: 'claude-opus-4-8' cannot be used as an advisor when the request model is 'claude-fable-5'.
issues.what_happened: The user's first `/dummyindex-build brand-centric-migration` invocation died instantly with this 400 from the API; the user had to run `/advisor` ('Advisor disabled') and retype the whole command. Root cause is Claude Code's advisor feature being pinned to claude-opus-4-8 right after the user switched the session model to Fable 5 — not a dummyindex defect — but it added a failed attempt and a manual retry to the dummyindex workflow. Occurred once.
issues.root_cause_guess: Claude Code harness advisor configuration incompatible with the newly selected request model; unrelated to dummyindex except as workflow friction. Included only to explain the duplicate /dummyindex-build invocation in the transcript.

summary: Short (~4 minute) session in the BOS-Mono backend repo running `/dummyindex-build brand-centric-migration` against a 27-item proposal. The dummyindex CLI itself executed cleanly (`--version`, `--status`, `--next --json` all succeeded), but the workflow stalled at item 1: the matcher confidently assigned a pure database-migration/RLS-decision GATE to the generic `python-implementer` agent — despite the prior session (per the recap in this transcript) having hand-built db-manager/Data Engineer agents because equip's own output needed manual fixing — and the user interrupted immediately after seeing that match, so nothing was built. Secondary friction: the per-repo skill copy (0.15.0, old serial-loop instructions) had drifted from the CLI (0.15.3) and the divergence was noticed but not remediated, the SessionStart drift report miscounted/triplicated drift for one edited file, and the first invocation was wasted on an unrelated Claude Code advisor-model API error.

issues.title: dummyindex versions sprawled across 5+ install locations; user had to run a dedicated cleanup session
issues.evidence: User: "remove dummyindex all other versions of dummyindex other than 0.22" ... Assistant inventory: "`~/.local/lib/python3.12/...` | **0.7.0** | stray user-pip ... `backend/.venv` (this repo) | **0.19.2** | dev dep pinned `>=0.15`; the shadowing copy from your memory note | `Projects/memory/dummyindex/.venv` | **0.20.0** ... `~/.cache/uv/archive-v0/...` | 0.4.31 → 0.21.0 (dozens)" — plus a previously hand-trashed binary at /home/ahmed/.local/share/Trash/files/dummyindex.
issues.what_happened: The entire session was the user asking Claude to hunt down and delete every dummyindex copy except 0.22.0. Claude found four distinct stale installs (pip user-site 0.7.0, backend venv 0.19.2, dummyindex editable 0.20.0, dozens of uv-cache archives 0.4.31–0.21.0) plus a binary the user had previously dragged to Trash by hand. Cleanup took the whole 61-message turn (~228s of agent work) across uv lock/sync, two uninstalls, cache prune/clean, and manual rm of cache dirs.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex has no single canonical install channel: it has been installed over time via pip --user, uv tool (twice: home + snap-rev path), as a project dev dependency, and as editable dev installs. The update path (/dummyindex-update detects ONE channel and reinstalls it) never uninstalls or even reports copies in the other channels, so stale versions accumulate and shadow each other until the user manually purges.

issues.title: dummyindex pinned as a project dev dependency creates a stale in-venv CLI (0.19.2) shadowing the global 0.22.0
issues.evidence: pyproject.toml:66:    "dummyindex>=0.15",  ... uv.lock: name = "dummyindex" / version = "0.19.2" ... backend .venv bin: /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/backend/.venv/bin/dummyindex ... Assistant: "dev dep pinned `>=0.15`; the shadowing copy from your memory note" and "a plain delete gets undone by the next `uv sync` since it's a dev dep."
issues.what_happened: The BOS-Mono backend repo carries dummyindex as a dev dependency with a loose `>=0.15` pin, locked at 0.19.2 — three minor versions behind the global CLI. Anyone running dummyindex inside the activated venv (or via `uv run`) gets 0.19.2 behavior (pre-folder-restructure, pre-parallel-council) while believing they are on 0.22. Simply deleting the venv copy would be reverted by the next `uv sync`, so the fix required `uv lock --upgrade-package dummyindex` + `uv sync`.
issues.root_cause_guess: Wiring dummyindex into a repo as a loose-pinned dev dependency (likely done at equip/install time) decouples the in-repo CLI version from the globally updated tool; nothing in dummyindex's update or drift-report flow checks or bumps the per-repo lock, so per-repo copies silently go stale.

issues.title: Verification of the active CLI failed: `dummyindex --version` / `command -v dummyindex` returned empty in non-interactive shells (exit 1)
issues.evidence: Command: "echo \"=== active CLI ===\" && which dummyindex && dummyindex --version 2>/dev/null || dummyindex version 2>/dev/null" → result: "=== active CLI ===" (empty). Retry: "command -v dummyindex && /home/ahmed/.local/bin/dummyindex --version" → "Exit code 1 ... === active CLI ===" (empty). Assistant: "`command -v dummyindex` returned empty inside this non-interactive shell because `~/.local/bin` isn't on its `PATH`".
issues.what_happened: Twice (lines 62-63 and 66-67) Claude tried to confirm the surviving CLI's version and got nothing — the session ended with the version never actually verified by execution, only inferred from dist-info directory names. The second attempt even used the absolute path /home/ahmed/.local/bin/dummyindex --version but short-circuited on `command -v` failing first, exiting 1.
issues.root_cause_guess: ~/.local/bin is absent from the non-interactive Bash PATH (the known snap-rev PATH-churn gotcha for this machine), and the install layout (pip user-site script + separate snap uv-tool shim) means the binary's resolvability depends on shell type. dummyindex offers no robust self-report path (e.g., `python -m dummyindex --version`) that the docs steer toward for verification.

issues.title: DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH Stop hook is built to fail silently — and likely no-ops on this machine due to the same PATH gap
issues.evidence:
Stop hook command (from stop_hook_summary): "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH
command -v dummyindex >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0
dummyindex context memory nudge --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true
exit 0
" — durationMs 25, hasOutput false; same session showed `command -v dummyindex` returns empty in non-interactive shells.
issues.what_happened: At session stop, the dummyindex memory-nudge hook ran for 25ms and produced no output. The hook's first line bails out silently if `command -v dummyindex` fails — which this very session demonstrated is the case in non-interactive shells on this machine (~/.local/bin not on PATH). Every error path is also swallowed (`2>/dev/null || true`), so the handoff-nudge feature can be silently disabled indefinitely with no signal to the user.
issues.root_cause_guess: Hook guard depends on PATH resolution of the dummyindex binary instead of a recorded absolute path, combined with total error suppression. On machines where non-interactive PATH lacks ~/.local/bin (snap PATH churn), the nudge never fires and nothing reports it.

issues.title: uv cache polluted with dozens of old dummyindex builds; `uv cache clean dummyindex` left 14 editable-install dirs needing manual rm -rf
issues.evidence: "=== remaining in uv cache === dummyindex-0.12.0 ... dummyindex-0.21.0 dummyindex-0.4.31" after `uv cache prune` (6.3GiB removed) AND `uv cache clean dummyindex` ("Removed 1692 files (9.9MiB)"). Assistant: "A few unpacked archive fragments survived `clean` ... pure-dummyindex editable-install cache entry (only its dist-info + editable finder/.pth)" — then 14 hash dirs removed by hand.
issues.what_happened: The 48GB uv cache contained unpacked archives for at least 13 distinct dummyindex versions (0.4.31 through 0.21.0), most of them editable-install entries. Neither `uv cache prune` nor the targeted `uv cache clean dummyindex` removed them; Claude had to identify each content-addressed archive-v0/<hash> dir as pure-dummyindex and rm -rf 14 of them individually.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex's dev/update workflow (`uv tool install --force --editable <repo>` on every version bump, per the user's own notes) creates a new content-addressed editable cache entry per version that uv's cleaning commands don't associate with the package name. High release cadence (0.4→0.22) multiplies the debris; dummyindex provides no uninstall/cleanup verb that handles this.

issues.title: Duplicate parallel 0.22.0 installs remain even after cleanup (pip user-site + snap-revision uv tool)
issues.evidence: Post-cleanup state: "/home/ahmed/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/dummyindex-0.22.0.dist-info | /home/ahmed/snap/code/247/.local/share/uv/tools/dummyindex/lib/python3.13/site-packages/dummyindex-0.22.0.dist-info" — with active shim `#!/usr/bin/python3.11` at ~/.local/bin/dummyindex and `uv tool list --show-paths` pointing at the snap path.
issues.what_happened: After the purge, two independent full installs of 0.22.0 still coexist: one pip-style user-site install under python3.11 (the PATH-active one) and one uv-tool install under a snap-revision-specific directory (~/snap/code/247/...). Claude kept both because both were 0.22, but they will diverge on the next update of either channel — recreating the exact sprawl just cleaned up. (Counted once; the snap path itself churns per snap revision.)
issues.root_cause_guess: uv-tool installs done from inside a snap-packaged environment land in revision-scoped dirs, while at some point a pip --user install created a second channel. dummyindex's install/update tooling detects only one channel and has no dedupe/consolidate step, so duplicate channels persist by design.

issues.title: Orphaned dummyindex 0.7.0 in system-python user-site required --break-system-packages to remove
issues.evidence: First uninstall attempt blocked: "note: If you believe this is a mistake ... You can override this, at the risk of breaking your Python installation or OS, by passing --break-system-packages. hint: See PEP 668" — then "Found existing installation: dummyindex 0.7.0 / Successfully uninstalled dummyindex-0.7.0" via `python3.12 -m pip uninstall -y --break-system-packages dummyindex`.
issues.what_happened: A very old 0.7.0 copy sat in ~/.local/lib/python3.12 (system python user-site) from an early pip-based install. PEP 668 blocked the normal uninstall, forcing Claude to use the risky --break-system-packages override on the OS python.
issues.root_cause_guess: Early dummyindex versions were installed with `pip install --user` against the distro python; when the project later switched to uv-tool installs, the old copy was orphaned with no migration/uninstall step, and removing it now requires overriding distro protections.

summary: Short (84-line, single-task) session in the BOS-Mono backend repo where the user asked Claude to "remove dummyindex all other versions of dummyindex other than 0.22". No plugin wiring, equip, or .context/reconcile operations occurred, so none of those failure modes appear here — but the session is itself a symptom: dummyindex's install/update lifecycle had littered the machine with five-plus stale copies (pip user-site 0.7.0, project-venv 0.19.2 shadowing the CLI, an editable 0.20.0, dozens of uv-cache archives, a hand-trashed binary), and even after cleanup two parallel 0.22.0 channels remain. Claude performed the cleanup competently (with one user interruption to re-ask scoping questions), though version verification via `dummyindex --version` never actually succeeded due to non-interactive PATH gaps — the same gap that likely makes the DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH Stop hook a silent no-op.

issues.title: SessionStart drift report flags dummyindex's own equip-generated agent file as unreconciled drift
issues.evidence:
## .context/ drift report

### New files not yet in any feature

1 file(s) added since the index was last reconciled are owned by no feature. The reconcile procedure decides where each belongs (a new feature, or attached to an existing one).

- `.claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md`

_New/unenriched code is a commit-anchored signal — it clears only when you reconcile. Run `/dummyindex --recouncil` (see `council/65-reconcile.md`): place new files, (re-)enrich, then `reconcile-stamp` the anchor._
issues.what_happened: At session start the drift hook reported drift whose only content was an untracked `.claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md` — a specialist agent file produced by dummyindex's own equip workflow, not application source. The report tells the user to run the full reconcile procedure (place into a feature, re-enrich, reconcile-stamp) just to clear a generated toolkit file. The prior session's handoff had ended with `has_drift: False`, so equip output created fresh 'drift' immediately. Seen once in this session (every new session in this repo would re-show it until reconciled).
issues.root_cause_guess: The drift/new-file scanner has no exclusion for dummyindex-managed paths (`.claude/agents/`, `.claude/skills/`, `.context/` itself). Equip-generated artifacts should be claimed by equipment.json / hash baseline or ignored by the commit-anchored new-file detector, not routed into the feature-placement reconcile flow.

issues.title: CLI upgrade 0.19.2→0.22.0 trashed the editable install and left skill files at the old version
issues.evidence: (2) **CLI upgraded 0.19.2→0.22.0** mid-session (old editable `~/.local/bin/dummyindex` trashed, reinstalled 0.22.0); skill files at `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` still say 0.19.2 — run `dummyindex install --scope user` to sync if desired (everything worked despite the mismatch). Backend venv also has the 0.22.0 binary at `backend/.venv/bin/dummyindex`.
issues.what_happened: The session-memory handoff injected at SessionStart records that during the previous session the dummyindex CLI upgrade broke the existing editable install (binary 'trashed', had to be reinstalled), and the upgrade did NOT sync the `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` skill family — leaving a 0.19.2 skill driving a 0.22.0 CLI across three minor versions. A second stray 0.22.0 binary also ended up in `backend/.venv/bin/dummyindex`. The mismatch persisted into this session (PR #44 stat at L28 shows `.claude/skills/dummyindex/.dummyindex_version` committed at the stale version).
issues.root_cause_guess: Upgrading the package does not atomically update the skill layer; `dummyindex install` is a separate manual step the user must remember. The /dummyindex-update three-layer flow either wasn't used or didn't exist on that version; uv-tool/editable installs are fragile across upgrades and multiple binaries (user PATH vs project venv) can shadow each other.

issues.title: Reconcile/equip outputs chronically left uncommitted in the working tree across sessions
issues.evidence: Next: (1) `.context/` reconcile is UNCOMMITTED (~18 files, working tree) — commit it. [...later, git status in this session:] M .claude/agents/frontend-reviewer.md | M .claude/agents/typescript-implementer.md | M .claude/agents/typescript-tester.md | M .claude/skills/frontend-verify/SKILL.md | M .context/equipment.json | ?? .claude/agents/frontend-performance-specialist.md | ?? .context/conventions/design-tooling.md | ?? .context/proposals/
issues.what_happened: The prior session's handoff explicitly flagged ~18 uncommitted reconcile files as the top follow-up, and in this session the working tree still held modified equip-generated agents/skills, a modified `.context/equipment.json`, and untracked `.context/conventions/` + `.context/proposals/` artifacts. The user's only question this session was whether their committed work was safe on `development` — the dummyindex artifacts scattered across the tree were the part not anywhere. Recurs across at least two sessions.
issues.root_cause_guess: Neither the reconcile procedure nor equip ends with a commit step (or even a strong prompt to commit), so index/toolkit state drifts from the repo history and accumulates as loose working-tree changes — matching the recent dummyindex docs fix 'emphasize committing index as dedicated commits'.

summary: A very short session (one user turn) in BOS-Mono/frontend on branch chore/dummyindex-context-reconcile: the user asked whether all committed changes were on development, and Claude correctly verified the branch was squash-merged as PR #44 with identical trees. dummyindex itself was not actively driven — its Stop-hook nudge and SessionStart hooks ran without errors — so no CLI failures or plugin wiring/install attempts occurred live. The struggles in evidence are ambient: the drift report flagged dummyindex's own equip-generated specialist agent as unowned drift demanding a reconcile, the injected session-memory documented a CLI upgrade that trashed the editable install and left the skill family three versions stale, and reconcile/equip outputs remained uncommitted across sessions.

issues.title: dummyindex-generated .context/map/files.json sha256 hashes trip detect-secrets and fail CI (~140 hits)
issues.evidence:
ERROR: Potential secrets about to be committed to git repo!

Secret Type: Hex High Entropy String
Location:    .context/map/files.json:11

Secret Type: Hex High Entropy String
Location:    .context/map/files.json:20
[... ~140 more files.json hits ...]
Secret Type: Hex High Entropy String
Location:    .context/source-docs/INDEX.json:41

Secret Type: Hex High Entropy String
Location:    .context/source-docs/INDEX.json:98
issues.what_happened: User pasted a failing GitHub Actions pre-commit run (`uv run pre-commit run --all-files --config .pre-commit-config-ci.yaml`). The detect-secrets hook flagged roughly 140 'Hex High Entropy String' locations, virtually all in committed dummyindex output: .context/map/files.json (per-file `"sha256": "a4c3095ab01..."` content hashes) and .context/source-docs/INDEX.json. The assistant verified they were dummyindex's own content hashes, not secrets, then had to exclude the whole .context/ tree from the hook. The hook also looped on '.secrets.baseline is unstaged' until the refreshed baseline was staged.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex embeds high-entropy sha256 content hashes in committed JSON artifacts and neither documents nor wires any detect-secrets exemption (baseline entry, pragma, or exclude) when it installs into a repo that has secret scanning. Since hashes regenerate on every rebuild, baselining is futile — the generator should emit scanner-safe output or the installer should add the exclude itself.

issues.title: ~40 generated .context/ files fail trailing-whitespace and end-of-file pre-commit hooks

issues.what_happened: The same CI run failed two basic hygiene hooks because ~40 committed dummyindex artifacts (_reality-check.md, docs.md, feature.json, INDEX.json/md, flow-*.md, symbol-graph.json, proposal plan.md, plus the vendored skill's council file and .dummyindex_version) have trailing whitespace or lack a final newline. Because the files are regenerated wholesale, one-time fixes would regress on the next rebuild, so the assistant excluded the trees from pre-commit instead of fixing the files.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex's markdown/JSON writers don't normalize output to common lint conventions (strip trailing whitespace, ensure POSIX final newline). Any consumer repo with standard pre-commit-hooks gets red CI the moment .context/ is committed.

issues.title: Council-generated spec.md uses realistic 'ghp_...' API-key placeholder, flagged as Secret Keyword
issues.evidence:
Secret Type: Secret Keyword
Location:    .context/features/integrations-oauth/spec.md:153

[file content at that line:]
3. `PUT /v1/integrations/<id>/credentials` with `{"api_key": "ghp_..."}` → `IntegrationCredentialsResponse` with `has_api_key: true`.
issues.what_happened: detect-secrets flagged a Secret Keyword inside a dummyindex council-generated feature spec — an example request body using a GitHub-PAT-shaped placeholder (`"api_key": "ghp_..."`). The assistant confirmed it was a docs placeholder and it got swept into the blanket .context/ exclude.
issues.root_cause_guess: Council prose generation writes example payloads with realistic secret prefixes and no `pragma: allowlist secret` annotation; generated docs should use clearly fake, scanner-safe placeholders.

issues.title: Only viable remedy was blanket pre-commit exclusion of .context/ and the vendored skill — generated trees now fully unlinted
issues.evidence:
# Generated trees (dummyindex .context output + vendored skill) are regenerated
# wholesale, so cosmetic and entropy hooks churn on them endlessly — skip them.
exclude: ^(\.context/|\.claude/skills/dummyindex/)

[assistant summary:] "Rather than one-time-fixing files that would regress on the next index rebuild, I added a global `exclude: ^(\.context/|\.claude/skills/dummyindex/)` to both `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and `.pre-commit-config-ci.yaml`."
issues.what_happened: To get CI green, the session added a global exclude for both dummyindex trees to BOTH pre-commit configs — meaning check-json, merge-conflict detection, large-file checks and secret scanning now skip .context/ and .claude/skills/dummyindex/ entirely. This downstream workaround had to be invented per-repo by the consuming session.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex's install/wiring step doesn't detect an existing pre-commit/secret-scanning setup and doesn't ship the needed excludes (or scanner-safe output) itself, so every equipped repo with CI lint hits this wall and resolves it ad hoc — losing all linting on the generated trees as collateral.

issues.title: Past dummyindex/tester agent sessions left dropped stashes and dangling commits on chore/dummyindex-context-index; user had to run a recovery audit

The audit found six unreachable stash-commits (tester-wip, tester-wip2, plus index/untracked-file stashes) dropped from a dummyindex working branch, containing real test-file edits (7 files, 66 insertions/118 deletions) and a .context/ proposal spec.md enrichment. They turned out to be superseded by a later commit only by luck of timing; the user spent a whole verification pass confirming nothing was lost. dummyindex-era work was also scattered across four branches (mcp-port, chore/dummyindex-context-index, backend-rework, development) as WIP commits ('WIP: MCP port with dummyIndex').
issues.root_cause_guess: Equip-generated tester/implementer agent sessions ran concurrently on a shared checkout, stashing WIP under the dummyindex context branch and later dropping those stashes instead of committing — the known concurrent-worktree hazard. dummyindex's agent workflow has no rule to commit (or worktree-isolate) WIP before branch switches, so multi-session work scatters and nearly loses changes.

issues.title: Committed index artifacts churn massively — symbol-graph.json diff of 113,596 lines between consecutive days
issues.evidence:

 .context/map/files.json            |    372 +-
 .context/map/symbols.json          |   4214 +-
[...]

issues.what_happened: Comparing a June 9 stash to the June 10 branch tip showed the committed .context/ backbone rewrote ~59k lines, dominated by symbol-graph.json (113,596-line diff) and map/symbols.json (4,214 lines). This made the lost-work audit slow, bloats every commit that includes a rebuild, and is the same wholesale-regeneration property that forced the pre-commit excludes ('regenerated wholesale, so cosmetic and entropy hooks churn on them endlessly').
issues.root_cause_guess: Deterministic rebuild rewrites giant monolithic JSON artifacts (likely with unstable ordering or full re-serialization) rather than producing minimal stable diffs; committing these to git makes history and review unusable around index updates.

issues.title: Drift accumulated to 66 source files across 12 features; SessionStart report ignored for the whole session
issues.evidence:
## .context/ drift report

66 source files across 12 features have been edited since the matching `.context/features/<id>/` docs were last touched. If your current task overlaps any of these features, review and update the relevant docs (`spec.md`, `plan.md`, `concerns.md`, `flows/*.md`)...

_Drift clears naturally: once you edit a feature doc, its mtime updates and these entries drop off._
issues.what_happened: The SessionStart hook ran fine and reported very large drift (66 files / 12 of the repo's features, including mcp-server and integrations-oauth which this session then touched via tests/core/mcp edits). The session never updated any .context/ doc, never ran rebuild/reconcile, and the drift only grew. Nothing in the workflow nudged again after the opening banner (Stop hook fired 3x with level='suggestion', no output, never surfaced drift).
issues.root_cause_guess: Report-only drift with no actionable follow-through: the wall-of-text banner at session start is easy to ignore mid-task, and the mtime-based 'drift clears naturally' framing invites touching docs rather than reconciling content. (The later v0.23.0 reconcile-gate addresses this; this session predates it and shows exactly why it was needed.)

issues.title: Vendored skill copy committed into the consumer repo gets edited there and is subject to repo CI
issues.evidence:
Fixing .claude/skills/dummyindex/council/10-structural-review.md
[...]
Fixing .claude/skills/dummyindex/.dummyindex_version

[branch listing:] mcp-port 4eedb09 [origin/development: ahead 2, behind 1] Add new council phases for reality checks, flow refinement, tree enrichment, and context lookups using dummyIndex
6ddcbc6 chore: add dummyindex .context index and Claude config
issues.what_happened: The dummyindex skill family (council phase files, .dummyindex_version) is vendored and committed inside the consumer repo's .claude/skills/dummyindex/, so its formatting violations broke the repo's CI, and a consumer-repo commit ('Add new council phases for reality checks, flow refinement, tree enrichment, and context lookups using dummyIndex') shows the vendored copy being developed/diverged inside the app repo rather than upstream. The fix was to exclude the whole vendored tree from pre-commit.
issues.root_cause_guess: Install strategy copies the skill into each repo and commits it; that copy then drifts from upstream, collides with the repo's lint/secret tooling, and tempts in-repo edits to dummyindex internals that never flow back. A global (~/.claude) install or gitignored vendored tree would avoid both.

summary: Session in the BOS-Mono/backend repo (a dummyindex-equipped consumer project) did two things: (1) a git-archaeology audit after the user feared work from prior agent sessions was lost — uncovering dropped tester-wip stashes and WIP commits scattered across a chore/dummyindex-context-index branch, and (2) fixing a red CI pre-commit run whose failures were dominated by dummyindex-generated artifacts (.context/ sha256 hashes flagged as secrets ~140 times, ~40 files failing whitespace/EOF hooks, a ghp_ placeholder in a council spec). dummyindex's CLI and skills were never directly invoked and no plugin discover/install/wire activity occurred, so no plugin-specific struggles appear here; the struggles are all with dummyindex's committed artifacts and agent-session workflow — the resolution was to blanket-exclude .context/ and the vendored skill from all pre-commit hooks, and the 66-file drift report was ignored throughout.

issues.title: User manually cycles /plugin and /reload-plugins right after dummyindex plugin installs — wiring apparently not live without a manual reload
issues.evidence: L9 18:58:46 "/plugin" -> "(no content)"; L13 19:26:07 "/plugin" -> "(no content)"; L17 19:26:14 "/reload-plugins" -> "Reloaded: 9 plugins · 12 skills · 45 agents · 5 hooks · 3 plugin MCP servers · 1 plugin LSP server"; L21 19:26:44 "/plugin" -> "(no content)"
issues.what_happened: This entire session (branch chore/dummyindex-context-reconcile, opened minutes after the prior session where dummyindex natively installed/wired the canvas-to-code and impeccable design plugins) consists ONLY of the user opening the interactive plugin manager three times and forcing a /reload-plugins in between — no prompts to Claude at all. The pattern (check /plugin, check again 27 min later, force reload, check once more) strongly suggests the user was verifying whether dummyindex-installed plugins actually appeared and had to manually reload to pick them up. Inferential: /plugin UI content is not captured in the transcript.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex's `equip install` writes plugin wiring into .claude/settings.json / settings.local.json on disk, but Claude Code only picks the change up at session start or after an explicit /reload-plugins — the install verb doesn't trigger or instruct a reload, leaving the user to discover plugins are 'missing' and manually poke the plugin manager.

issues.title: Inconsistent plugin wiring targets: one plugin wired into settings.json, the other into settings.local.json
issues.evidence: Verified both design plugins work through dummyindex: **canvas-to-code** (settings.json, equipment→`canvas-to-code:canvas-to-code-pm`, grounded in `playbooks/canvas-to-code.md`+`design-workflow.md`) and **impeccable** (settings.local.json, skill, grounded in `playbooks/impeccable.md`+`design-workflow.md`).
issues.what_happened: The SessionStart session-memory handoff (injected into this session) records that of two design plugins installed/wired via dummyindex in the prior session, canvas-to-code landed in .claude/settings.json (shared/committed) while impeccable landed in .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored, per-machine). The handoff also notes a prior session had to explicitly 'verify both design plugins work through dummyindex', implying doubt that the wiring took. Inconsistent targets mean one plugin travels with the repo and the other silently doesn't.
issues.root_cause_guess: The install/wire code path chooses settings.json vs settings.local.json non-deterministically (e.g., different trust tier or different install verb branch) instead of one documented, consistent target — so team members or future sessions get half the wired plugins.

issues.title: Mid-session CLI upgrade trashed the editable install and left skill files at 0.19.2 while the CLI moved to 0.22.0 (unsynced version skew)
issues.evidence: **CLI upgraded 0.19.2→0.22.0** mid-session (old editable `~/.local/bin/dummyindex` trashed, reinstalled 0.22.0); skill files at `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` still say 0.19.2 — run `dummyindex install --scope user` to sync if desired (everything worked despite the mismatch).
issues.what_happened: The prior-session handoff (carried into this session at SessionStart) records that upgrading the CLI destroyed the existing editable install at ~/.local/bin/dummyindex mid-session, and that the upgrade did NOT update the repo's skill procedure files, which still claim 0.19.2 three versions behind the 0.22.0 binary. The user is left to remember to run `dummyindex install --scope user` manually; the skew persisted across at least two sessions (it is restated in core-memories.md: 'The skill procedure files under `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` may still read `0.19.2`').
issues.root_cause_guess: CLI upgrade and skill-file installation are separate, manually-coordinated layers: upgrading the package neither preserves editable installs nor re-runs `dummyindex install` to sync skills/wiring, so Claude can be driven by 0.19.2-era SKILL.md instructions against a 0.22.0 CLI.

issues.title: Auto-clustering produced 734 unusable Leiden communities; rebuild/ingest can re-shatter the hand-curated 13-feature taxonomy
issues.evidence: collapsed 734 Leiden communities into the **13-feature taxonomy** via `scaffold-feature` ... "The `.context/` index uses a **13-feature taxonomy** ... hand-collapsed from 734 raw Leiden communities by file path. Features were built via `scaffold-feature` from path-based file lists, so they carry no auto-detected flows. If a future `ingest`/`rebuild` re-shatters the repo into `community-N` clusters, re-apply this regrouping rather than councilling the raw communities."
issues.what_happened: The handoff/core-memories injected at SessionStart show the deterministic ingest produced 734 raw community-N clusters for this frontend repo — so unusable the user had to hand-collapse them into 13 features by file path. Worse, the user had to write a standing defensive memory warning future sessions that a routine `ingest`/`rebuild` may destroy that curation and re-shatter the taxonomy, and that path-scaffolded features 'carry no auto-detected flows'.
issues.root_cause_guess: The Leiden community-detection backbone over-fragments large frontend codebases and has no mechanism to persist or respect a user-curated feature taxonomy across rebuilds — rebuild treats clustering output as authoritative instead of reconciling into the existing curated grouping.

issues.title: dummyindex's own generated tooling files show as 'unowned' drift, forcing --force on reconcile-stamp
issues.evidence: The `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` tooling files are intentionally owned by no feature; the commit anchor was force-stamped past them — they show as "unowned" in drift reports. Not a problem. (Once committed/behind the anchor they drop out of `unassigned_new_files` — a clean `reconcile-stamp` then needs no `--force`.)
issues.what_happened: Core memory (injected at SessionStart) records that files dummyindex itself installs into the repo (.claude/skills/dummyindex/**) are flagged by its own drift report as unowned/unassigned_new_files, and the user had to force-stamp the commit anchor past them. The user wrote a permanent memory explaining 'Not a problem' so future sessions don't chase the false positive.
issues.root_cause_guess: The drift/reconcile machinery has no exclusion list for dummyindex's own generated artifacts, so the tool reports its own installation as code drift and requires a --force escape hatch.

issues.title: Commit-anchored reconcile broke when a rebase rewrote history, requiring a manual full reconcile/re-enrich pass
issues.evidence: After a rebase rewrote history (old anchor `52f4fa6c` → now `92c96bb5`; new commit `d263a8bf` PR#43 "typography redesign from Jun-10 feedback", +297 lines in brand-hub) I reconciled `.context/` to the new HEAD. ... Ran `rebuild --changed` (clean, schema stayed v1) then `reconcile-stamp` → anchor=`92c96bb5`, **has_drift: False**.
issues.what_happened: The handoff from the prior session shows the .context/ commit anchor pointed at a SHA (52f4fa6c) invalidated by a rebase, and recovering required a manual multi-step pass: re-enrich brand-hub spec.md+plan.md, touch up three other features, run rebuild --changed, then reconcile-stamp to re-anchor at 92c96bb5. Recovery worked but cost a whole session segment of manual reconciliation.
issues.root_cause_guess: The Model-B commit anchor stores a raw SHA with no resilience to history rewrites (no reflog/merge-base fallback), so any rebase orphans the anchor and dumps the full reconcile burden on the user/agent.

issues.title: Reconcile leaves ~18 .context files uncommitted; committing spills into a dedicated chore branch in the next session
issues.evidence: Next: (1) `.context/` reconcile is UNCOMMITTED (~18 files, working tree) — commit it. ... "gitBranch":"chore/dummyindex-context-reconcile"
issues.what_happened: The prior session's reconcile finished with ~18 modified .context/ files sitting uncommitted in the working tree; the handoff's top 'Next' item is to commit them, and this session is indeed on a dedicated chore/dummyindex-context-reconcile branch created to do that. The reconcile loop doesn't close itself with the documented dedicated commit, leaving curated index state at risk in a shared working tree.
issues.root_cause_guess: The reconcile skill/procedure stops at stamping and treats committing as out-of-band, despite docs emphasizing committing the index as dedicated commits — so the final step routinely leaks across sessions.

issues.title: Duplicate dummyindex binaries (user PATH copy plus backend venv copy) create version-skew risk
issues.evidence: Primary launcher: `~/.local/bin/dummyindex` (on PATH, so the SessionStart drift hook works). A second copy lives in the backend venv: `backend/.venv/bin/dummyindex`.
issues.what_happened: Core memory records two independent dummyindex binaries on the same machine/repo (the PATH-installed launcher the SessionStart hook depends on, plus a copy inside backend/.venv). The user has to track which one is primary and notes the hook only works because the PATH copy exists — a fragile arrangement that already coincided with the 0.19.2→0.22.0 skew event.
issues.root_cause_guess: Installation has no single canonical location or self-check; installing into a project venv silently duplicates the CLI, and the SessionStart hook resolves whichever binary PATH order yields.

summary: This is a nearly empty 25-line session (2026-06-11, BOS-Mono/frontend, branch chore/dummyindex-context-reconcile) in which the user never prompted Claude — they only opened the interactive /plugin manager three times and ran /reload-plugins once, a pattern consistent with manually verifying/forcing pickup of plugins dummyindex had installed the session before. All concrete dummyindex evidence comes from the session-memory handoff and core-memories injected by the SessionStart hook, which document prior-session struggles: a mid-session CLI upgrade that trashed the editable install and left skill files three versions stale, inconsistent plugin wiring targets (settings.json vs settings.local.json), a rebase-broken commit anchor requiring manual reconcile, the tool flagging its own generated files as drift (forcing --force), 734 unusable auto-detected communities that had to be hand-collapsed into 13 features with rebuild threatening to re-shatter them, and an unfinished reconcile commit spilling into this dedicated branch. No dummyindex CLI invocation occurred (or failed) within this session itself.

issues.title: Repo-scoped dummyindex forces manual cross-repo handoff: user must paste a /dummyindex-plan prompt into the backend repo themselves
issues.evidence: USER: "finish up everythong on frontend and then give me a prompt to plan using dummy-index i can run on backend" ... ASSISTANT: "Run this **inside `/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/backend`** (it has its own `.context/` index). Paste exactly:" ... [earlier tool result] "Shell cwd was reset to /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/frontend"
issues.what_happened: The session ran in BOS-Mono/frontend but the user's actual goal was backend work in the sibling repo BOS-Mono/backend (both have their own .context/). After ~2 turns of friction the user gave up on having this session do the backend work and explicitly asked for a paste-able prompt instead. The assistant authored a ~3000-char /dummyindex-plan prompt for the user to run manually in a new session inside the backend repo. The backend's own .context/ index was never consulted either — all backend exploration was raw cat/grep of source. Every cd into the backend was also undone by the harness cwd reset.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex-plan/build/equip are strictly cwd-repo-scoped with no --root/path targeting for a sibling repo (only the base /dummyindex skill advertises a <path> arg). In a monorepo-of-repos layout there is no supported way to plan against repo B from a session in repo A, so the workflow degrades to 'write a prompt, switch sessions, paste'.

issues.title: Plan→build→reconcile pipeline marked feature done while shipping a flagged-but-unfixed wiring bug (landing card hard-coded to 0)
issues.evidence: Code comment: "Illustrations is a placeholder module in v1 — empty route, no real source. Static values match the Claude Design reference" ... ASSISTANT: "`useBrandHubModules` holds `artImages` (same shared pool) but calls `deriveIllustrationsModule()` with no args, so the landing card hard-codes \"0 ILLUSTRATIONS / Get started\" even when the library is full. That's a genuine wiring bug."
issues.what_happened: The brand-hub-illustrations feature had already been driven through the dummyindex proposal pipeline (commits: proposal added, feature built, 'docs(context): reconcile illustrations into brand-hub + tests features'). Yet the shipped result left the landing-page Illustrations card statically showing '0 ILLUSTRATIONS / Get started' regardless of content — a gap the bridge doc itself flagged as 'caveat 3' but the build loop never closed before reconciling/closing out. This session had to rediscover and fix it manually (2 edits + 3 new tests).
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex-build's completion criterion is the checklist, not the spec's flagged caveats: items noted in side docs (bridge spike 'caveat 3') or marked placeholder never become checklist items, so the loop reconciles and stamps a feature 'done' with known wiring gaps still open.

issues.title: Plan-stage deferrals (critique-panel 'Decision #5: DEFERRED to v2') conflicted with user intent — user demanded 'nothing defered'
issues.evidence: Spec (quoted by assistant): "**Decision #5:** Version history DEFERRED to v2 — 'the version feed is `kind`-scoped and can't scope to `variant=illustration`. Revisit when the backend can scope `/assets/versions` by `variant`.'" ... USER: "i need it too be 100% functional nothing defered, with ability to upload, download, view illustrations and all screens wired correctly"
issues.what_happened: The dummyindex-plan spec (revised after its critique panel) deliberately scoped the feature to 'zero new backend surface' and deferred version history, cross-format download, and variant filtering to v2. The user clearly did not share that scoping — when asked to 'update backend' they pushed back: '100% functional nothing defered'. The deferred items became this session's entire remediation workload (and the backend plan prompt's 4 gap items).
issues.root_cause_guess: The plan skill's critique panel optimizes for minimal scope and records deferrals as spec 'Decisions' without an explicit user sign-off step, so deferrals silently become product gaps the user discovers later; there is also no follow-up artifact (e.g. a v2 proposal stub) tracking what was deferred.

issues.title: .context spec treated as authority over the user's explicit request — 3 restatements, a rejected AskUserQuestion, and an interrupt before work started
issues.evidence: ASSISTANT: "there's a genuine fork I need you to settle before I touch backend code — because the design docs explicitly say the backend needs **no** changes, yet you're asking me to update it." USER turn 1: "update /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/backend for brand-hub-illustrations"; turn 2: "i need you to update backend accordinlgy"; turn 3: "i need it too be 100% functional nothing defered..."; then "[Request interrupted by user]"
issues.what_happened: Grounded in the .context proposal/spec saying 'no backend changes needed', the agent repeatedly pushed back on the user's direct instruction instead of executing: a 19-minute stall after reading the bridge doc (user had to type 'Continue from where you left off', got 'No response requested.'), then a multi-option AskUserQuestion the user rejected, then more tracing until the user interrupted and downgraded the goal to 'just give me a prompt'. The user restated the same request 3 times across the session.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex's grounding docs carry strong normative language ('out of scope', 'Decisions') and the skill guidance lacks a rule for spec-vs-user conflicts (the CLAUDE.md only covers code-vs-index conflicts: 'the code wins'). When the index contradicts the USER, the agent litigates the spec instead of treating the user as the override.

issues.title: Documented .context navigation path skipped: HOW_TO_USE.md/INDEX.md never read, no `dummyindex query`; agent navigated by find/grep
issues.evidence: First .context access of the session: "find .context -path '*illustration*' -o -name '*.md' -path '*proposal*' 2>/dev/null | grep -i illustr; echo \"---\"; find .context -path '*brand-hub*' -name 'spec.md'" — no Read of .context/HOW_TO_USE.md or INDEX.md and zero `dummyindex` CLI invocations appear anywhere in the transcript.
issues.what_happened: Despite both the global and project CLAUDE.md mandating 'read .context/HOW_TO_USE.md before any non-trivial task' and the skill advertising deterministic `query` retrieval, the agent never read HOW_TO_USE.md/INDEX.md and never ran the dummyindex CLI once in the whole session — it located proposal/spec docs via shell find/grep. It worked here, but it means the indexed navigation layer added no enforced value and the documented entry path is being ignored.
issues.root_cause_guess: The HOW_TO_USE entry-point convention is enforced only by CLAUDE.md prose with no hook or skill nudge; once the agent knows the .context layout from memory/handoff, the documented path is cheaper to skip than to follow.

issues.title: Three-way dummyindex version skew across layers and repos (skill files 0.19.2, frontend CLI 0.22.0, backend 0.24.0)
issues.evidence: SessionStart handoff memory: "**CLI upgraded 0.19.2→0.22.0** mid-session (old editable `~/.local/bin/dummyindex` trashed, reinstalled 0.22.0); skill files at `.claude/skills/dummyindex/**` still say 0.19.2 — run `dummyindex install --scope user` to sync if desired (everything worked despite the mismatch). Backend venv also has the 0.22.0 binary at `backend/.venv/bin/dummyindex`." Backend git log: "5738c13 chore: update dummyindex to 0.24.0 + refresh .context backbone (#16)"
issues.what_happened: The session's own SessionStart memory surfaced an unresolved installer mess carried over from prior sessions: an editable CLI install got 'trashed' during upgrade, skill files were still two minor versions behind the CLI, a second copy of the binary lives inside the backend's venv, and the backend repo was meanwhile bumped to 0.24.0 — leaving at least three different dummyindex versions in play across layers/repos. The recommended sync (`dummyindex install --scope user`) was still not done as of this session.
issues.root_cause_guess: CLI upgrade and skill/wiring refresh are separate manual steps with no atomicity or post-upgrade check; per-repo/per-venv binary copies multiply the skew. The /dummyindex-update skill exists to fix exactly this but nothing nudges the user to run it when a mismatch is detected.

issues.title: Session ended with source edits never reconciled into .context/ — Stop hook only nudged about session-memory, no drift/reconcile gate fired
issues.evidence: Stop hook stdout: "dummyindex: this session is substantial (subagents: 0; ~90,770 main-thread output tokens) and no handoff has been saved this session. In one short line, offer the user the option to checkpoint a session handoff, and only if they agree run /dummyindex-remember. Do NOT save automatically." stop_hook_summary hooks: "# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH ... dummyindex context memory nudge --root \"$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR\"" + plugin stop-hook.sh only.
issues.what_happened: The session changed `deriveIllustrationsModule`'s signature/behavior in module-derivation.ts, edited useBrandHubModules.ts, and added tests — making .context/features/brand-hub docs stale — then ended uncommitted with no `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`/reconcile run and no prompt to do so. The only dummyindex Stop-hook output was the handoff nudge (which the user never answered); the always-on reconcile drift gate (v0.23+) is absent from this repo's hook wiring, consistent with the stale 0.19.2/0.22.0-era install. Hook ran on every stop (3x) but produced the nudge only once (correct dedupe).
issues.root_cause_guess: Per-repo hook wiring predates the v0.23 auto-council drift gate and was never refreshed (`dummyindex install` not re-run after CLI upgrades), so the index silently drifts after in-session code edits with no gate or nudge covering reconcile — only session-memory.

summary: Session in BOS-Mono/frontend (a dummyindex-indexed, equipped repo): the user asked to update the sibling backend repo so the brand-hub-illustrations feature is "100% functional nothing defered". dummyindex's .context artifacts grounded the agent well factually, but the session exposed workflow problems: the dummyindex-plan spec had deliberately deferred the backend pieces, so the agent litigated the spec against the user (3 restatements + an interrupt), and because dummyindex is repo-scoped the backend work ended as a manually paste-able /dummyindex-plan prompt for another session; the earlier build loop had also shipped a flagged-but-unfixed landing-card bug this session had to close. Notably, the dummyindex CLI was never invoked once, HOW_TO_USE.md was never read, version skew (0.19.2 skills / 0.22.0 CLI / 0.24.0 backend) persisted, and the session ended with code edits unreconciled into .context/. No plugin discover/install/wiring activity occurred in this transcript, so it provides no direct evidence on the owner's plugin-wiring hypothesis — only the Stop-hook handoff nudge, which behaved correctly.

Trivial 27-line session in the BOS-Mono monorepo: the user asked what dummyindex version both submodules (frontend/backend) were on, and Claude answered correctly by grepping the dummyindex_version stamp (0.25.0, schema 1) from each submodule's .context/meta.json. Everything succeeded — no dummyindex CLI calls, no errors, no hook failures, no drift-gate activity, and no user corrections — so the session shows NO dummyindex problems. Minor non-issue observation: frontend/.context/ has equipment.json but no equipment/ directory (backend has both), a structural asymmetry that caused no friction here.

issues.title: Global dummyindex directive misfires at BOS-Mono root: .context/ exists but has no HOW_TO_USE.md / INDEX.md / PROJECT.md / features/
issues.evidence: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md line 5: "When working in a repo that has a `.context/` folder at its root, **read `.context/HOW_TO_USE.md` before any non-trivial task** ... Navigate `.context/INDEX.md` → `.context/PROJECT.md` → `.context/features/<id>/` ... flag it and run `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`." — vs live state: "ls: cannot access '.context/HOW_TO_USE.md': No such file or directory" (same for INDEX.md, PROJECT.md, config.json, equipment.json). /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.context/ contains only docs/ and memory/; root CLAUDE.md line 20: ".context/ ← Docs + plain-markdown agent memory".
issues.what_happened: BOS-Mono root's .context/ is NOT a dummyindex index — it is the project's own pre-existing 'Dev Context Layer' (~100 curated, git-tracked product/research docs under .context/docs/ plus a team memory store under .context/memory/). dummyindex was never installed at the root (no managed CLAUDE.md block, no SessionStart drift hook in .claude/settings.json, no meta.json/config.json) — intentionally, since the repo's docs delegate dummyindex to the frontend/ and backend/ submodules. But the dummyindex-installed user-global directive keys on the mere existence of a `.context/` folder, so every Claude session opened at the mono root is told to read four files that don't exist, and the prescribed remedy (`dummyindex context rebuild --changed`) would scaffold a dummyindex backbone INTO the foreign curated docs tree if a session ever obeyed it.
issues.root_cause_guess: The global directive (written by `dummyindex install --scope user`) uses directory-name detection (`.context/` exists) instead of an ownership marker (e.g. presence of `.context/meta.json` or HOW_TO_USE.md). It assumes every `.context/` is dummyindex-owned.

issues.title: No foreign-.context ownership guard: preflight unconditionally claims `.context/**` as managed
issues.evidence: /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/context/domains/preflight/render.py: "_MANAGED_PATHS = (\".context/**\", \".claude/CLAUDE.md  (managed block only — surrounding content preserved)\", \".claude/settings.json  (one additive SessionStart hook)\")" — rendered under "## Will write / manage". No code path in preflight inventories or warns about an existing `.context/` that lacks `meta.json`.
issues.what_happened: Latent hazard made concrete by this repo: if anyone runs `/dummyindex` at BOS-Mono root (plausible, given the global directive in issue 1 points there), preflight would announce it will 'write / manage' `.context/**` — which at this root means the project's curated docs tree and team memory store — with no warning that the directory belongs to another system. Git log confirms it has not fired yet (no dummyindex writes ever landed in the root .context/), but the live state is one invocation away from polluting a tracked docs tree.
issues.root_cause_guess: Preflight inventories .claude/ (settings validity, rules, agents, CLAUDE.md block) and git cleanliness, but never reads `.context/meta.json` to verify ownership before promising to manage the tree. Needs a 'foreign .context detected (no meta.json) — refuse or require --force/alternate dir' check.

issues.title: Mono root memory file is lowercase .claude/claude.md while dummyindex and settings permissions target uppercase .claude/CLAUDE.md
issues.evidence: .claude/ listing shows only "claude.md" (8473 bytes, no CLAUDE.md); .claude/settings.json permissions: "Edit(.claude/CLAUDE.md)", "Write(.claude/CLAUDE.md)"; dummyindex preflight render.py managed path: ".claude/CLAUDE.md  (managed block only — surrounding content preserved)".
issues.what_happened: On this case-sensitive Linux filesystem the mono's per-directory guide is `.claude/claude.md` (lowercase). The repo's own settings.json permission allow-rules reference the uppercase path that doesn't exist, and dummyindex's managed-block writer hard-codes `.claude/CLAUDE.md` — so a root dummyindex run would create a second, parallel memory file beside the human-authored lowercase one instead of appending the managed block to it. Depending on Claude Code's case handling, the lowercase file may also not load as project memory at all, silencing the repo's 'thin mono-scope guide'.
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex's bootstrap writer does an exact-case path check for `.claude/CLAUDE.md` and never does a case-insensitive lookup for an existing claude.md before creating its own file.

issues.title: .context/memory/ namespace is occupied by a foreign memory system — validates dummyindex's session-memory collision dodge
issues.evidence: /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.context/memory/README.md: "# Codebase Context Memory — Plain-markdown memory for the codebase context layer. This is our **internal dev memory**..." with shared/ (committed) + local/ (gitignored) tiers in entities/patterns/preferences/events/cases/profile categories.
issues.what_happened: BOS-Mono's own agent-memory store lives at exactly `.context/memory/` — the path dummyindex deliberately avoided by choosing `.context/session-memory/`. The dodge worked (no clash on disk; submodules keep their session-memory inside their own .context/), but any dummyindex code path, doc, or future regression that touches `.context/memory/` would clobber a foreign, partially git-tracked store. Worth a regression test in dummyindex.
issues.root_cause_guess: Not a defect today; a confirmed real-world instance of the namespace hazard already recorded in the project's design notes.

issues.title: disabledMcpjsonServers lists 'framer' but .mcp.json defines no framer server
issues.evidence: .claude/settings.json: "disabledMcpjsonServers": ["framer", "notion"] — while .mcp.json mcpServers defines only: supabase, vercel, figma, sentry, notion, linear, firecrawl.
issues.what_happened: Stale disable entry pointing at a server that no longer exists in the project .mcp.json ('notion' is still valid). Harmless but confusing config drift. Not dummyindex-produced — project-owned settings.
issues.root_cause_guess: framer server was removed from .mcp.json without cleaning the disable list.

issues.title: Eight vendored plugins under .claude/plugins/ are not wired in enabledPlugins; README presents them as live capabilities
issues.evidence: .claude/settings.json: "enabledPlugins": { "karimo@karimo": true } — yet .claude/plugins/ contains agent-sdk-dev, code-review, commit-commands, feature-dev, hookify, plugin-dev, pr-review-toolkit, ralph-wiggum; .claude/plugins/README.md inventory table advertises "`/new-sdk-app`", "`/hookify`, `/hookify:list`", "`/plugin-dev:create-plugin`", "`/pr-review-toolkit:review-pr`".
issues.what_happened: Claude Code loads plugins from marketplaces declared in settings, not from a project's .claude/plugins/ folder, so only karimo (via the opensesh/KARIMO marketplace) is actually enabled here. The other vendored copies are inert reference snapshots that can silently drift from the user-scope installs that really serve those slash commands, while the README implies they're wired at this layer. Not dummyindex-produced, but relevant to the equip/plugin-manager mental model of 'installed = wired in settings.json'.
issues.root_cause_guess: Plugins were vendored for readability/version-pinning before the team adopted marketplace-based enablement; README never updated to distinguish 'vendored for reference' from 'enabled'.

issues.title: .claude/agents/ holds no agents and its README links to a nonexistent directory
issues.evidence: .claude/agents/README.md inventory: "[feature-dev (agent)](./feature-dev%20(agent)/)" — actual directory is .claude/agents/feature-dev/ (no ' (agent)' suffix); the whole agents/ tree contains only README.md files. Meanwhile .claude/claude.md table claims "agents/ | Autonomous multi-step workflows (KARIMO implementer, tester, PM, and more)" — those agent files actually live in .claude/plugins/karimo/agents/.
issues.what_happened: Cosmetic doc rot: the agents directory advertised in two places contains zero agent definitions, and the one inventory link is URL-encoded to a path that doesn't exist. Any dummyindex preflight run here would correctly report 'project agents: none', contradicting the repo's own docs.
issues.root_cause_guess: Agents were moved into the vendored karimo plugin (and the feature-dev agent dir renamed) without updating the README inventories.

issues.title: Blanket Write(./**) / Edit(./**) permissions make the five narrower allow rules redundant
