issues.evidence: .claude/settings.json permissions.allow: ["Write(./**)", "Edit(./**)", "Edit(.claude/CLAUDE.md)", "Write(.claude/CLAUDE.md)", "Edit(.karimo/.worktrees/**/.claude/CLAUDE.md)", "Write(.karimo/.worktrees/**/.claude/CLAUDE.md)", "Edit(**/.claude/CLAUDE.md)", "Write(**/.claude/CLAUDE.md)", ...]
issues.what_happened: The first two rules allow writing/editing every file in the repo, so the six CLAUDE.md-specific rules (which also reference the uppercase path that doesn't exist at this root — see the case-mismatch issue) are dead config. Duplicate/conflicting permission entries, cosmetic only.
issues.root_cause_guess: Narrow rules were added first for KARIMO worktrees, then the blanket rules were added later without pruning.

summary: Audited the live on-disk state at the BOS-Mono monorepo root. The root `.context/` is NOT a dummyindex index and never was — it is the project's own pre-existing 'Dev Context Layer' (curated docs/ + a team memory store), with zero dummyindex artifacts (no HOW_TO_USE.md, INDEX.md, PROJECT.md, features/, meta.json, config.json, or equipment.json), no managed CLAUDE.md block, and no dummyindex hooks in settings.json; dummyindex is intentionally scoped to the git submodules, and both frontend/.context and backend/.context carry full healthy indexes (HOW_TO_USE.md + equipment.json present, and the equipment usage docs the root onboarding doc references actually exist). The real dummyindex problems here are wiring-level: the user-global CLAUDE.md directive fires on any `.context/` folder and so misroutes every root session to nonexistent files (and prescribes a rebuild that would pollute the foreign docs tree), and the tool's preflight has no foreign-`.context/` ownership guard — it unconditionally claims `.context/**` as managed. Versions are current (CLI 0.25.0 = skill stamp 0.25.0); remaining findings are project-owned config drift (stale framer MCP disable, unwired vendored plugins, lowercase claude.md vs uppercase write targets, doc-rotted agents README).

issues.title: equip apply/add-specialist rewrites equipment.json from scratch and silently drops marketplace plugin install records
issues.evidence: git show 696c932 -- .context/equipment.json removes: '{ "kind": "agent", "name": "superpowers@claude-plugins-official", "path": ".claude/settings.json", "source": "marketplace", ... "grounded_in": [".context/equipment/superpowers.md"], "marketplace": "claude-plugins-official", "origin_repo": "anthropics/claude-plugins-official", "mechanism": "native" }'. Current /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/backend/.context/equipment.json has no plugin entry, yet .claude/settings.json still has 'enabledPlugins: {"superpowers@claude-plugins-official": true}' and the playbook .context/equipment/superpowers.md exists.
issues.what_happened: The superpowers plugin was installed via equip (manifest entry + usage playbook + native settings wiring all present at commit a7be5db). A later `equip add-specialist` run (commit 696c932, which added backend-docs/security specialists) rebuilt the manifest and the plugin record vanished, while the plugin stayed enabled in settings.json. Result on disk now: `dummyindex context equip status --json` lists only the 6 generated items and reports "missing_playbook": [] — the plugin manager is blind to a plugin it installed, and `equip uninstall` would leave it enabled (breaking the skill's promise: 'Every install is recorded in .context/equipment.json ... so status / uninstall cover marketplace and vendored items').
issues.root_cause_guess: In /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/cli/equip/dispatch.py `_apply_write()` builds the new manifest as `EquipmentManifest(items=tuple(written))` where `written` = rendered items + decision.adopt + hook items. Prior manifest entries with source 'marketplace' (and vendored items) are never carried forward — only generated baselines and re-derived adoptions survive a re-apply.

issues.title: Tree enrichment (Phase 4.5) wiped by re-ingest: all 1117 tree.json abstracts are EXTRACTED stubs in a standard-mode repo
issues.evidence: Live .context/tree.json: Counter({'EXTRACTED': 1117}) — every node abstract is a stub like 'Directory at app/ (6 immediate children).' Git history: `git show 6d7c31f:.context/tree.json | grep -c INFERRED` = 196; next commit touching it, 6a4cab6 'WIP: MCP port with dummyIndex', = 0. .context/config.json has "mode": "standard".
issues.what_happened: Phase 4.5 enrichment originally ran (196 INFERRED node abstracts committed). A later ingest/rebuild regenerated tree.json and destroyed all enriched abstracts; enrichment was never re-run. The skill promises standard mode enriches the structure batch so 'a future session's PageIndex walk over tree.json reads real prose' — live retrieval reads only stubs, and nothing (drift hook, reconcile-gate, status) flags the loss. This also contradicts the dummyindex-update skill's 'NON-DESTRUCTIVE deterministic backbone rebuild — never touching curated .context taxonomy' framing.
issues.root_cause_guess: tree.json is treated as deterministic backbone, so ingest/rebuild regenerates it wholesale instead of preserving INFERRED abstracts for unchanged nodes; no post-rebuild check reports enrichment regression.

issues.title: SessionStart drift hook and Stop reconcile-gate are invisible to parent-repo (BOS-Mono root) sessions — submodule-only wiring
issues.evidence: backend/.claude/settings.json wires '# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH ... dummyindex context plan-update --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"' (SessionStart) and 'dummyindex context reconcile-gate --root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"' (Stop). The parent /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.claude/settings.json contains no dummyindex hooks (its SessionStart runs scripts/git-mode.sh), while BOS-Mono/CLAUDE.md says 'dummyindex is each submodule's workflow + plugin manager. Feature work in a submodule goes through ... /dummyindex-plan → /dummyindex-build'.
issues.what_happened: Claude Code only loads .claude/settings.json from the session's project root. The parent monorepo clearly hosts sessions at its root (it has its own .claude/ with agents, hooks, skills and a CLAUDE.md that mandates the dummyindex workflow for submodules), but for such sessions the backend submodule's drift report, memory hooks, and the always-on Stop reconcile-gate never fire — backend edits from a mono-root session bypass the gate silently. The hooks only work when a session is opened with backend/ itself as cwd. dummyindex has no parent-level wiring story for submodules.
issues.root_cause_guess: Hook installer assumes session root == indexed repo root; for a submodule it would need a parent-scope hook (or documented limitation) that invokes `dummyindex context plan-update/reconcile-gate --root <submodule>` per indexed submodule.

issues.title: supabase plugin enabled in settings.local.json with no equipment.json record and no usage playbook; equip status claims nothing is missing
issues.evidence: .claude/settings.local.json: '{ "enabledPlugins": { "superpowers@claude-plugins-official": true, "supabase@claude-plugins-official": true } }'. .context/equipment/ contains only superpowers.md; `git log --all -S supabase -- .context/equipment.json` returns nothing; `dummyindex context equip status --json` outputs '"missing_playbook": []'.
issues.what_happened: A second marketplace plugin (supabase) is live in the repo (local scope) but was never recorded in the equipment manifest and has no .context/equipment/supabase.md playbook, so per the equip skill it should show as 'incomplete' — instead status reports a clean bill because missing_playbook is computed only from manifest items (dummyindex/context/domains/equip/lifecycle/status.py), never from the actual enabledPlugins in settings files. Whether supabase was wired by hand or by equip, the live state violates 'a plugin with no playbook shows incomplete in equip status'.
issues.root_cause_guess: status/missing_playbook derives solely from .context/equipment.json instead of reconciling against enabledPlugins in .claude/settings*.json, so out-of-band (or dropped) installs are invisible.

issues.title: equipment.json grounded_in for backend-security-specialist points at three nonexistent files
issues.evidence: .context/equipment.json, backend-security-specialist item: '"grounded_in": [... ".context/conventions/auth.md", ".context/conventions/security.md", ".context/DECISIONS.md"]'. On disk, .context/conventions/ contains only coding-practices.md, data-access.md, folder-organization.md, naming.json, naming.md, testing.md; ls .context/DECISIONS.md → 'No such file or directory' (the repo's real decisions doc is .context/docs/DECISIONS.md).
issues.what_happened: The security specialist's manifest grounding records auth.md, security.md and DECISIONS.md that do not exist in this repo, and misses the actually-existing .context/docs/DECISIONS.md. The rendered agent file hedges with '(if present)', but the manifest's grounded_in — the audit trail of what the tool is grounded in — is wrong, and any tooling that trusts it (or an agent that tries to read those paths) hits dead links.
issues.root_cause_guess: grounding_docs are hardcoded constants in /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/context/domains/equip/generate/specialists.py (lines 87-97: '.context/conventions/auth.md', '.context/conventions/security.md', _DECISIONS='.context/DECISIONS.md') and are recorded into the manifest without an existence filter against the target repo.

issues.title: Duplicate dummyindex skill family at project + user scope, with diverging dummyindex-build semantics under the same 0.25.0 stamp
issues.evidence: backend/.claude/skills/ contains full copies of dummyindex, dummyindex-audit/-build/-equip/-plan/-remember/-update; backend/.claude/skills/dummyindex/.dummyindex_version = 0.25.0 = user copy. diff shows dummyindex-build differs: the user copy (~/.claude/skills/dummyindex-build/SKILL.md) adds 'Substitution is a build failure. If the tagged tool is unavailable ... never let an agent hand-write the output' plus a canvas-to-code provenance gate; the project copy matches the shipped 0.25.0 package source exactly (grep -c 'Substitution is a build failure' on dummyindex/skills/build/SKILL.md in the dummyindex repo = 0).
issues.what_happened: The same seven skills are registered twice in this repo (project scope wins in Claude Code). The user-scope dummyindex-build was hand-strengthened with binding '— via' routing rules that never made it into the dummyindex package, so in this repo the older, weaker project copy shadows the intended semantics — agents may silently hand-implement '— via'-tagged checklist items, the exact failure the user-scope edit forbids. Both copies present as 'Installed from dummyindex 0.25.0', masking the divergence.
issues.root_cause_guess: An improvement was edited directly into the installed user skill instead of the package source; dummyindex install/update has no checksum drift detection between scopes, and the version banner is the only (insufficient) freshness signal.

issues.title: Committed .context/config.json carries a machine-absolute external_docs path reaching outside the submodule
issues.evidence: .context/config.json: '"external_docs": ["/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.context/docs"]' (committed; changed in submodule commits). .context/meta.json similarly commits '"root": "/mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/backend"'.
issues.what_happened: The onboarding-persisted external docs root is an absolute path on this machine pointing at the parent monorepo's .context/docs. Any other clone/CI/teammate of the backend submodule gets a dangling external_docs reference (and a wrong meta.json root) until they re-onboard. For a submodule the reference should be relative (e.g. ../.context/docs) or declared unresolvable-portably.
issues.root_cause_guess: onboard/ingest persists user-supplied paths verbatim with no relativization or portability warning for paths outside the indexed root.

issues.title: superpowers plugin enabled redundantly in both settings.json and settings.local.json
issues.evidence: .claude/settings.json: '"enabledPlugins": { "superpowers@claude-plugins-official": true }' and .claude/settings.local.json: '"enabledPlugins": { "superpowers@claude-plugins-official": true, ... }'.
issues.what_happened: The same plugin is enabled at project scope (committed, the equip-managed location) and again at local scope. Harmless today, but disabling/uninstalling via the project file leaves the local override re-enabling it, and it muddies which scope owns the install.
issues.root_cause_guess: A native/manual enable (local) overlapped an equip native install (project); equip does not detect or dedupe cross-scope enables.

issues.title: Generated ruff-format hook quotes $CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS as a single argument
issues.evidence: .claude/settings.json PostToolUse hook: 'ruff format "$CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS" 2>/dev/null || true'.
issues.what_happened: CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS can be a space-separated list; quoting it as one argument makes ruff receive a single nonexistent path for multi-file tool calls, and the '|| true' swallows the failure, so formatting silently no-ops in that case (single-file edits work). It also runs ruff against non-Python files (errors suppressed).
issues.root_cause_guess: Equip's hook template treats CLAUDE_FILE_PATHS as a single path; needs word-splitting or a per-path loop with a *.py filter.

summary: Audited the live dummyindex-produced state in the BOS-Mono/backend git submodule against the dummyindex/dummyindex-equip skill specs and the dummyindex source. The core .context/ taxonomy is healthy (HOW_TO_USE/INDEX/PROJECT/conventions/18 consistent features, all JSON parses, version stamps aligned at 0.25.0, anchor sane, hooks installed and functional inside the submodule). The serious failures are in the equip plugin-manager bookkeeping and the update path: equip's apply/add-specialist rewrote equipment.json and dropped the recorded superpowers marketplace install (leaving a wired-but-untracked plugin that status/uninstall can no longer see), a re-ingest destroyed all 196 enriched tree.json abstracts leaving 100% stubs in a standard-mode repo, and the SessionStart drift hook + Stop reconcile-gate are wired only inside the submodule so parent-root sessions bypass them entirely. Secondary issues: phantom grounded_in paths, duplicate diverging skill installs across scopes, an unrecorded supabase plugin, and a non-portable absolute external_docs path.

issues.title: 0.25.0 install/rebuild re-shattered the curated 14-feature taxonomy into 838 phantom community-N features (committed)
issues.evidence: frontend/.context/features/INDEX.json (HEAD): 838 features, first feature_id 'community-0'; 0 of the 838 listed dirs exist on disk, while all 14 real curated dirs (account, app-shell, auth, brain, brand-hub, chat, design-system-base, home, home-v3, projects, shared-lib, shared-ui-custom, tests, tooling) are absent from the index. Commit 4477cef6: "revert(claude): drop auto rebuild --changed hooks — 0.25.0 re-shatters the curated index ... `rebuild --changed` re-clusters the repo into ~838 raw community-N features instead of preserving the curated 13-feature taxonomy (0.24.0 preserved it)". HEAD 0509e767: "0.25.0 re-shatters curated index (confirmed live)".
issues.what_happened: Commit 29d029b3 ('chore(claude): sync dummyindex skill to 0.25.0', via `dummyindex install`) replaced the curated features/INDEX.json (14 features at 29d029b3~1) with 838 raw Leiden 'community-N' stubs and committed it. The curated feature DIRS survived on disk but the index/INDEX.md/graph artifacts no longer reference them, so every retrieval path the docs prescribe ('Start at features/INDEX.json') routes agents into 838 nonexistent dirs. .context/INDEX.md 'Files in this run' lists features/community-0/docs.md etc. — none exist. .context/session-memory/core-memories.md had pre-warned: 'If a future ingest/rebuild re-shatters the repo into community-N clusters, re-apply this regrouping'. The user had to revert auto-rebuild hooks (4477cef6) and strip `rebuild --changed` from proposal preflight (0509e767) to defend the index — twice.
issues.root_cause_guess: 0.25.0's rebuild/install path runs a full deterministic re-cluster (or a version-migration ingest) instead of preserving the existing features/ taxonomy on disk; the dummyindex-update skill promises a 'NON-DESTRUCTIVE deterministic backbone rebuild — never touching curated .context/ taxonomy' but the 0.25.0 implementation regenerates features/INDEX.json from raw graph communities, ignoring curated dirs created via scaffold-feature/features-rename.

issues.title: equip apply --for-proposal rewrote equipment.json and silently dropped 5 previously tracked items (specialists, marketplace plugin, vendored skills)
issues.evidence: git show 2742f514:.context/equipment.json items: [typescript-implementer, typescript-tester, frontend-reviewer, frontend-verify, frontend-docs-specialist, frontend-security-specialist, frontend-performance-specialist, prettier-format, canvas-to-code@canvas-to-code-marketplace (source=marketplace), paper-design (installed), design-handoff (installed)]. git show 3333b758:.context/equipment.json (and HEAD): docs-specialist, performance-specialist, canvas-to-code, paper-design, design-handoff are GONE; search-specialist and a second 'frontend-reviewer (installed)' appeared. Skill spec: 'a plain `equip` re-run afterward **preserves** it (never silently drops a specialist you added)' and 'Every install is recorded in `.context/equipment.json`'.
issues.what_happened: Between commits 2742f514 and 3333b758 ('add art-direction-paper-rebuild proposal + equip toolkit'), the manifest was regenerated from scratch. The dropped items still exist live: .claude/agents/frontend-docs-specialist.md (version 1.1.0 — it had even been patch-bumped) and frontend-performance-specialist.md both carry `<!-- dummyindex:generated -->`, canvas-to-code@canvas-to-code-marketplace is enabled in .claude/settings.json, and skills paper-design/ + design-handoff/ are on disk — yet none are in equipment.json, so equip status/refresh/uninstall are now blind to them. Usage docs at .context/equipment/canvas-to-code.md and paper-design.md are orphaned, and frontend/CLAUDE.md's claim 'Both tools are registered as **dummyindex equipment**' is false against the live manifest.
issues.root_cause_guess: equip apply (the --for-proposal path) writes the manifest as a fresh render of the current plan's coverage instead of merging with the existing manifest, losing every item the current run didn't itself produce — exactly the 'never silently drops' contract violation.

issues.title: Duplicate conflicting 'frontend-reviewer' entries in equipment.json; adopted entry violates the path:"" contract
issues.evidence: /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/frontend/.context/equipment.json has two items named frontend-reviewer: one {"source": "generated", "path": ".claude/agents/frontend-reviewer.md", "version": "1.0.0", "origin_hash": "sha256:55ef..."} and one {"source": "installed", "path": ".claude/agents/frontend-reviewer.md", "capabilities": ["frontend", "review"], "origin_hash": null}. Skill spec: 'ADOPTED specialist — a manifest-only pointer (`"path": ""`, `"source": "installed"`, no `origin_hash`) ... **No file is written**'.
issues.what_happened: The same agent file is claimed twice with conflicting lifecycle semantics — one entry says equip owns it (hash-baselined, refreshable), the other says it's an externally 'installed' agent that was adopted. The adopted entry also carries a non-empty path pointing at the generated file, breaking the documented manifest-only (`path: ""`) contract for adoptions. Lifecycle verbs (status/refresh/reset/uninstall) and capability resolution now have two contradictory records for one path.
issues.root_cause_guess: The --for-proposal coverage resolver adopted the existing 'frontend-reviewer' to cover a 'frontend' capability gap without checking that the name/path was already a generated item in the same manifest, and filled the adoption's path from the discovered file instead of leaving it empty.

issues.title: Regenerated docs still instruct the confirmed-destructive command: HOW_TO_USE.md and the managed CLAUDE.md block tell every session to run `rebuild --changed`
issues.evidence: .context/HOW_TO_USE.md (regenerated 2026-06-13 02:57): 'It is generated by dummyIndex and refreshed by `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`.' Managed block in .claude/CLAUDE.md: 'when the index disagrees with the code, the code wins: flag it and run `dummyindex context rebuild --changed`.' Meanwhile the repo's own HEAD commit: 'Do **NOT** run `dummyindex context rebuild --changed` — confirmed live (2026-06-13): on 0.25.0 it re-shatters the curated feature taxonomy into auto `community-N` clusters'.
issues.what_happened: dummyindex's own generated guidance (the v0.25.0 HOW_TO_USE.md and the managed CLAUDE.md block, also embedded in the user's global ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) directs every future agent session to run the exact command the user has now proven destroys the curated index. Any obedient session that notices drift will re-trigger the re-shatter; the user is reduced to scattering DO-NOT-RUN warnings in proposals and commit messages to fight the tool's own docs.
issues.root_cause_guess: Doc templates hardcode `rebuild --changed` as the safe refresh path; they were not updated when 0.25.0's rebuild became taxonomy-destructive (or the rebuild regression simply wasn't caught before release).

issues.title: equipment.json grounded_in and generated specialist bodies reference four nonexistent .context files
issues.evidence: equipment.json frontend-security-specialist grounded_in includes '.context/conventions/auth.md', '.context/conventions/security.md', '.context/DECISIONS.md'; frontend-search-specialist includes '.context/docs/SCHEMA.md', '.context/DECISIONS.md'. ls: all four 'No such file or directory'. Agent bodies repeat them: frontend-security-specialist.md:24 '2. `.context/conventions/auth.md` and `.context/conventions/security.md`'; frontend-search-specialist.md:23 '2. `.context/conventions/data-access.md` and `.context/docs/SCHEMA.md`'.
issues.what_happened: The generated security and search specialists are 'grounded' in files dummyindex never generated for this repo (only DECISIONS.md is softened with '(if present)'; the conventions/SCHEMA references are unconditional mandatory-read steps). Agents dispatched from these specialists burn their grounding phase failing to read phantom paths, and the manifest's grounded_in — supposed to record real grounding — is partly fiction.
issues.root_cause_guess: Specialist templates (security/search) carry a fixed grounded_in list assuming a fuller .context layout (auth/security conventions, docs/SCHEMA.md, DECISIONS.md) instead of filtering the list against files that actually exist at render time.

issues.title: Drift report's 'new files not yet in any feature' flags dummyindex's own generated tooling and design artifacts as unowned code
issues.evidence: Live `dummyindex context plan-update --root .../frontend` output: 'New files not yet in any feature ... `.claude/agents/frontend-search-specialist.md` `.claude/commands/audit-devprops.md` ... `.claude/commands/paper-design.md` `.claude/reference/design-system.md` `.claude-design/brand-hub/illustrations/paper/iter-02-recommended/screenshots/01-illustrations-recommended.png`',
issues.what_happened: The SessionStart drift hook tells every session that equip's own generated agents/commands, plus .claude-design screenshots/yaml, are 33 'files owned by no feature' that 'the reconcile procedure decides where each belongs'. This nudges sessions to fold dummyindex's tooling and binary design captures into the code feature taxonomy — noise at best, taxonomy pollution at worst.
issues.root_cause_guess: The commit-anchored new-file scan diffs the whole tree since the anchor without excluding .claude/, .claude-design/ and other non-source tool directories from feature-ownership candidacy.

issues.title: Submodule wiring: hooks correct inside frontend/, but the BOS-Mono parent root has zero dummyindex wiring, so monorepo-rooted sessions bypass the drift report and reconcile gate
issues.evidence: frontend/.claude/settings.json wires SessionStart (plan-update, memory session-start), Stop (memory nudge, reconcile-gate), PreCompact, PostToolUse prettier — all with `--root "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"`. Parent /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/opensession/BOS-Mono/.claude/settings.json has its own SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks (git-mode.sh, docs-frontmatter-guard.mjs) and no DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH entries; BOS-Mono/.context is a different, non-dummyindex folder ('Docs + plain-markdown agent memory', no HOW_TO_USE.md).
issues.what_happened: Inside the submodule everything works — plan-update ran live, the gitdir-pointer .git file is handled, the anchor commit (1b7fa81f) resolves and is an ancestor of HEAD. But the documented workflow ('mounted at ../backend/ when inside the BOS-Mono monorepo') means sessions are routinely rooted at BOS-Mono, where frontend/.claude/settings.json is never loaded: no drift report, no Stop reconcile-gate, no managed CLAUDE.md block for the indexed submodule. The index drifts silently for exactly the sessions most likely to edit across frontend+backend. Also, the parent's unrelated `.context/` collides with the global-CLAUDE.md rule 'read .context/HOW_TO_USE.md' (the file doesn't exist there).
issues.root_cause_guess: dummyindex install wires hooks only at the indexed root and has no submodule/monorepo story (e.g. offering to add a parent-scoped hook with --root pointing at the submodule, or detecting that the indexed root is a submodule and warning).

issues.title: Plugin 'impeccable' enabled in settings.local.json with zero equip tracking — no manifest entry, no usage doc
issues.evidence: frontend/.claude/settings.local.json: {"enabledPlugins": {"impeccable@impeccable": true}, "extraKnownMarketplaces": {"impeccable": {"source": {"source": "github", "repo": "pbakaus/impeccable", "ref": "3.5.0"}}}}. equipment.json has no impeccable item in any revision; .context/equipment/ contains only canvas-to-code.md and paper-design.md. A playbook exists at .context/playbooks/impeccable.md instead.
issues.what_happened: An installed code-bearing third-party plugin (untrusted source pbakaus/impeccable) is live in local scope but invisible to the equip lifecycle: `equip status` cannot even show it as 'incomplete' because no record exists, `uninstall` would not disable it, and its usage knowledge landed in playbooks/ rather than the spec'd .context/equipment/<plugin>.md location. Per the equip skill, 'Every install is recorded in `.context/equipment.json` with its upstream origin ... and mechanism'.
issues.root_cause_guess: Either the plugin was wired outside `equip install` (by hand or another session) with no detection/adoption path in equip, or an equip install to --scope local wrote settings.local.json but the later manifest rewrite (see manifest-drop issue) erased the record.

issues.title: User had to hand-patch the in-repo dummyindex-build skill to make '— via <tool>' tags binding (anti-substitution + provenance gate) — uncommitted edit that the next install sync will clobber
issues.evidence: git diff .claude/skills/dummyindex-build/SKILL.md (working tree, modified): '+   **Honor `— via <tool>` tags — BINDING routing, not a hint.** ... **Substitution is a build failure.** If the tagged tool is unavailable, errors out, or can't complete, leave the item **unticked**, STOP, and report — never let an agent hand-write the output the tool was supposed to produce.' and '+   - **Design-to-code items (`— via canvas-to-code:start`) have a provenance gate.** ... A bridge run whose state shows no ingested source — or page code that appeared without a bridge run — is the exact no-source failure this gate exists to prevent'.
issues.what_happened: The shipped 0.25.0 build skill treats — via tags loosely enough that build agents hand-wrote outputs the tagged tool (canvas-to-code bridge) was supposed to produce — the repo's history shows a 'no-source illustrations UI' being removed (commit b395ce64) over exactly this failure. The user patched the project copy of the skill to forbid substitution and add a state-file provenance check. Because `dummyindex install` rewrites 'the in-repo skill family' (commit 29d029b3), this uncommitted hand-fix will be silently overwritten on the next skill sync, and the user-scope copy at ~/.claude/skills/dummyindex-build still has the weaker wording.
issues.root_cause_guess: The upstream build skill specifies — via tags as routing guidance without a failure semantics (what happens when the tool can't run), so agents fall back to hand-implementation; and the skill-sync path has no merge/preserve story for hand-edited in-repo skill copies.

issues.title: meta.json stamps stale/misleading after the 0.25.0 churn: updated_at predates the latest regeneration and created_at was reset by the reinstall
issues.evidence: .context/meta.json: {"created_at": "2026-06-12T19:17:42+00:00", "updated_at": "2026-06-12T19:34:56+00:00", "indexed_commit": "1b7fa81f...", "dummyindex_version": "0.25.0"} while HOW_TO_USE.md/INDEX.md/tree.json/meta.json mtimes are Jun 13 02:57–02:58 IST (~21:27 UTC Jun 12) — about two hours after updated_at.
issues.what_happened: The 02:57 'confirmed live' rebuild rewrote the backbone artifacts (and touched meta.json) without bumping updated_at, so the freshness stamp under-reports the last regeneration. created_at (2026-06-12T19:17) coincides with the 0.25.0 install at 00:46 IST Jun 13 — the 'non-destructive' upgrade reset the index's birth date, erasing ancestry back to the original ingest. Version stamps themselves are consistent (CLI 0.25.0 = skill family 0.25.0 = meta dummyindex_version 0.25.0), and indexed_commit is a valid ancestor of HEAD as the Model B anchor design intends.
issues.root_cause_guess: rebuild --changed rewrites meta.json fields selectively (or not at all) while regenerating siblings, and the install-triggered re-ingest recreates meta.json from scratch instead of preserving created_at.

summary: Audited the live dummyindex-produced state in the BOS-Mono/frontend git submodule (dummyindex 0.25.0 across CLI, skills, and meta stamp). The plumbing is largely present and functional — settings.json hooks are valid and all referenced CLI verbs exist, the SessionStart drift report runs correctly from inside the submodule, and the .context taxonomy has every required backbone file. But the 0.25.0 upgrade catastrophically re-shattered the curated 14-feature taxonomy into 838 phantom community-N entries (committed at 29d029b3, re-confirmed live at 02:57 on 2026-06-13), while the tool's own regenerated docs still instruct agents to run the destructive `rebuild --changed`. Separately, equip's manifest was silently rewritten during a --for-proposal apply, dropping five tracked items (two generated specialists still on disk, the canvas-to-code marketplace plugin, and two vendored skills) and introducing a duplicate/contract-violating frontend-reviewer adoption — breaking equip's hash-baselined lifecycle promise.

issues.title: Installed CLI payload is one commit behind repo HEAD while version strings match (false freshness signal)
issues.evidence: `dummyindex --version` -> `0.25.0`; pyproject.toml:7 `version = "0.25.0"`; CHANGELOG `## 0.25.0 (2026-06-12)`. But `git log origin/main..HEAD` -> `755e59b feat(skill): enhance documentation for plugin and skill discovery channels` (2026-06-13 04:29), and `diff -rq` repo vs install: `Files /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex/skills/equip/SKILL.md and /home/ahmed/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/dummyindex/skills/equip/SKILL.md differ`. Install timestamp 2026-06-13 00:36, i.e. before the commit.
issues.what_happened: The CLI is a pip --user COPY (dist-info INSTALLER=pip, ~/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages); it was reinstalled at 00:36 on Jun 13, then HEAD commit 755e59b (04:29) changed dummyindex/skills/equip/SKILL.md without a version bump (feat commit unpushed, so the release workflow never produced 0.26.0). Result: every version check passes (0.25.0 == 0.25.0 == 0.25.0) while the installed package content is stale by one commit. The skills' own freshness check ('Run dummyindex --version to confirm the CLI matches') cannot detect this class of staleness.
issues.root_cause_guess: Version-string comparison is the only freshness check, but the repo accumulates commits between release bumps (release automation only fires on push to GitHub; this feat commit is local-only). A content hash or commit-anchored stamp in the install would catch it; alternatively reinstall after every local feat commit.

issues.title: Installed dummyindex-equip skill missing the entire skills.sh / two-discovery-channels section from HEAD
issues.evidence: grep -c 'skills.sh': /home/ahmed/.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/SKILL.md -> 0; repo dummyindex/skills/equip/SKILL.md -> 9. Repo-only hunk: `+### Two discovery channels (know which you're reaching for)` ... `+- **Channel B — the open agent-skills ecosystem** (`npx skills`, backed by **https://skills.sh/**)` ... `+npx skills find [query]` (~150 added lines, plus new triggers `"find a plugin/skill for X"`, `"is there a plugin for X"` in the frontmatter description).
issues.what_happened: The live ~/.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/SKILL.md (installed 00:37:42) predates HEAD commit 755e59b (04:29) which added the Channel A (marketplace) vs Channel B (npx skills / skills.sh) discovery documentation and new trigger phrases. Any session invoking /dummyindex-equip today runs the old playbook: it cannot discover via skills.sh and won't trigger on the new phrases. All other skills (dummyindex, -audit, -plan, -remember, -update) match their repo sources exactly modulo the expected __VERSION__ -> 0.25.0 stamp; auxiliary dirs (council/, retrieval/, agents/, equip/templates/, audit/agents/) all match.
issues.root_cause_guess: Skill render happens at `dummyindex install` time from the installed package copy; the skill-doc commit landed after the last install and nothing re-ran `pip install` + `dummyindex install --scope user`. Fix: reinstall CLI from repo, re-run `dummyindex install --scope user` (or /dummyindex-update once the commit is pushed/released).

issues.title: Installed dummyindex-build skill locally diverged — hand edits exist only in ~/.claude and will be clobbered by the next install
issues.evidence: Installed-only diff lines (absent from repo dummyindex/skills/build/SKILL.md): `**Honor `— via <tool>` tags — BINDING routing, not a hint.**` ... `- **Substitution is a build failure.** If the tagged tool is unavailable ... never let an agent hand-write the output the tool was supposed to produce.` ... `- **Design-to-code items (`— via canvas-to-code:start`) have a provenance gate.** ... confirm the bridge's state file (`.canvas-to-code/state/<feature>/status.json`) records the run **with a non-null source iter**`. mtime 03:02:48 vs the 00:37:42 install batch of all other skills.
issues.what_happened: Some session edited /home/ahmed/.claude/skills/dummyindex-build/SKILL.md in place (~2.5h after install), strengthening the `— via` tag semantics from 'directive, not a hint' to 'BINDING routing' with a substitution-is-failure rule and a canvas-to-code provenance gate. None of this was ported back to the repo source (dummyindex/skills/build/SKILL.md) or the installed site-packages copy. The installer renders skills by overwriting SKILL.md, so the next `dummyindex install --scope user` / /dummyindex-update silently destroys these curated edits. Direction of fix matters: the content must flow upstream into the repo first. Secondary concern: the edits embed another project's plugin specifics (canvas-to-code) into the user-global skill, which now applies to every repo.
issues.root_cause_guess: No write-protection or hash-baseline on installed dummyindex skills (unlike equip-generated items, which have pristine/user-modified tracking) — installer overwrites unconditionally, and a session treated the installed skill as the editable source of truth.

issues.title: Help text drifted two releases behind the CLI surface: no `equip discover`/`equip install`, no `build --next-wave`
issues.evidence: cli/help.py:299: `build --proposal S (--next | --check "<item>" | --status) [--json]` — no --next-wave; yet cli/build_loop/dispatch.py:78 `want_wave = "--next-wave" in rest` and its error msg lists `--next, --next-wave, ...`. `dummyindex context --help | grep -E 'discover|equip install'` matches nothing except 'are discovered automatically', while cli/equip/dispatch.py routes `EquipVerb.DISCOVER` (line 96) and `EquipVerb.INSTALL` (line 98) and the equip skill documents `dummyindex context equip discover ...` / `equip install <plugin>@<marketplace> [--yes] [--scope project|local|user]`.
issues.what_happened: Every subcommand the skills reference exists and dispatches correctly (verified: context rebuild --changed, reconcile, reconcile-stamp, council-batch --next [--mode|--cap|--tree-enrich|--json], query, equip apply/add-specialist/status/refresh/reset/uninstall/patch, build --next-wave). But the v0.18.0 `build --next-wave` verb and the v0.19.0 `equip discover`/`equip install` plugin-manager verbs were never added to cli/help.py. An agent or user sanity-checking the skills against `--help` would wrongly conclude the wave/plugin-manager features don't exist in the installed CLI.
issues.root_cause_guess: help.py is a hand-maintained static string with no test asserting parity between dispatch verbs and documented verbs; the v0.18.0 and v0.19.0 PRs updated dispatch + skills but not help.py.

issues.title: Per-subcommand --help is rejected as an unknown argument instead of printing usage
issues.evidence: `dummyindex context equip --help` -> `error: unknown argument(s) for `equip`: ['--help']`; identical failures for `context build --help`, `context reconcile --help`, `context reconcile-stamp --help`, `context council-batch --help`.
issues.what_happened: Only `dummyindex --help` and `dummyindex context --help` work; the conventional `<subcommand> --help` errors out (exit non-zero). Skills instruct agents to run these subcommands, and the natural flag-discovery reflex fails, pushing agents to guess flags or grep source instead.
issues.root_cause_guess: Hand-rolled arg parsing in each verb handler treats --help as an unexpected token; no shared fallthrough that prints the relevant help.py section.

issues.title: Stale packaging artifacts in repo: dummyindex.egg-info pinned at 0.21.0 and an old build/lib tree
issues.evidence: /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex/dummyindex.egg-info/PKG-INFO: `Name: dummyindex` / `Version: 0.21.0` vs pyproject.toml `version = "0.25.0"`. Stale `build/bdist.linux-x86_64` + `build/lib/dummyindex/` tree also present. Both are gitignored (`.gitignore:6 *.egg-info/`, `.gitignore:9 /build/`).
issues.what_happened: Leftover setuptools artifacts from a 0.21.0-era build sit at the repo root, four releases stale. Harmless to git, but the egg-info advertises the wrong version to any setuptools/pkg_resources-style introspection run inside the repo, its SOURCES.txt no longer reflects the package, and a stale build/lib is the classic setuptools trap where deleted/renamed files get re-bundled into the next locally built wheel/sdist unless cleaned. Entry points themselves are consistent everywhere (pyproject [project.scripts], egg-info and dist-info all say `dummyindex = dummyindex.__main__:main`).
issues.root_cause_guess: A local `pip install .` / `setup.py`-style build at 0.21.0 left artifacts; no `rm -rf build *.egg-info` hygiene step in the release/install flow. Fix: delete both directories.

issues.title: Install-method drift: CLI is now a pip --user copy, not the recorded uv-tool install; broken `dummyindexy` uv receipt lingers
issues.evidence: `which dummyindex` -> /home/ahmed/.local/bin/dummyindex with shebang `#!/usr/bin/python3.11`; dist-info INSTALLER file contains `pip`; `uv tool list | grep -i dummy` -> empty, but stderr warns: `warning: Tool `dummyindexy` environment not found (run `uv tool install dummyindexy --reinstall` to reinstall)`. ~/.local/share/uv/tools/ contains only kimi-cli. From a neutral cwd, import resolves to the site-packages copy; from the repo root, `python3.11 -c "import dummyindex"` resolves to the repo tree (cwd shadowing).
issues.what_happened: The persisted gotcha ('uv-tool COPY; reinstall via uv tool install --force --editable <repo>') no longer matches reality: dummyindex is installed via pip --user into system python3.11 site-packages. Following the recorded reinstall advice would create a second, uv-managed install overwriting ~/.local/bin/dummyindex and could leave two divergent copies. A dead uv receipt for `dummyindexy` (the project's old name) emits warnings on every `uv tool list`. The cwd-shadowing gotcha (repo-root python masks installed-copy staleness) was re-confirmed live.
issues.root_cause_guess: Install method changed at the 00:36 Jun-13 reinstall (pip instead of uv tool) without updating the recorded gotcha or cleaning the orphaned `dummyindexy` uv receipt (`uv tool uninstall dummyindexy` or delete its receipt).

summary: State audit of the dummyindex install (CLI binary, site-packages copy, ~/.claude skills, packaging artifacts) against the repo at /mnt/windows-ssd/Projects/memory/dummyindex. The installed CLI reports 0.25.0, matching pyproject and the CHANGELOG, and every subcommand the skills reference (rebuild --changed, reconcile, reconcile-stamp, council-batch --next, query, equip discover/install/status, build --next-wave) actually exists and dispatches — so the core install is functional. However the installed payload is one unreleased commit behind HEAD (the equip skill's skills.sh discovery docs never reached the live skill), the installed build skill carries hand edits that exist nowhere in the repo and will be clobbered on next install, and the static help text is two releases behind the real verb surface (no equip discover/install, no build --next-wave). Packaging cruft (0.21.0 egg-info, stale build/, orphaned dummyindexy uv receipt) is cosmetic but worth cleaning.

issues.title: CLI closing command contradicts SKILL.md; Claude distrusts reconcile and never closes the loop
issues.evidence: CLI: 'all items checked — close the loop by reconciling the new code into .context/ (council/65-reconcile.md): dummyindex context reconcile' ... Claude to user: 'the build loop suggests dummyindex context reconcile to fold the new code into .context/ feature docs via the council. I held off (it can re-run clustering) — say the word if you want it run.'
issues.what_happened: Installed dummyindex-build SKILL.md (v0.15.0) says the closing step is rebuild --changed, but the CLI at 27/27 printed 'dummyindex context reconcile'. Claude, fearing reconcile would destructively re-cluster, declined to run it. Session ended with .context/features/* describing the pre-migration domain model after a complete domain rewrite.
issues.root_cause_guess: Skill doc and CLI output diverged across versions; nothing reassures the agent that reconcile is non-destructive — so the documented close-the-loop step gets skipped.

issues.title: rebuild --changed reports 'no feature drift detected' and skips feature regeneration after a sweeping domain migration, yet checklist items claiming regeneration get ticked
issues.evidence: 'context rebuild: enriched index preserved; refreshed 7 deterministic artefact(s) (no re-cluster). no feature drift detected.' — vs checklist item: 'dummyindex context rebuild --changed run; .context/features/* regenerated to the brand-centric reality.'
issues.what_happened: After a migration touching ~15 backend files, rebuild --changed refreshed only 7 deterministic artefacts and claimed no feature drift. Checklist items asserting features were 'regenerated' were ticked anyway — index left stale while marked done.
issues.root_cause_guess: Drift detection blind to semantic change inside already-indexed files; proposal generator writes acceptance text rebuild --changed cannot satisfy — only reconcile can, which was skipped.

issues.title: Plan/checklist GATE prescribes Supabase CLI ('supabase db push') that is not installed; Claude improvises hand-written baseline SQL and direct surgery on the prod migration-history table
issues.evidence: Checklist: 'GATE — Mechanism = Supabase CLI migrations (supabase/migrations/*.sql, supabase db push; DECISIONS §15), applied/tested via the Supabase MCP on a create_branch branch' ... probe result: 'no-supabase-cli no-supabase-dir'
issues.what_happened: The generated checklist hardwired the Supabase CLI + MCP mechanism, but no CLI was installed. Claude hand-derived a baseline migration from pg_catalog and directly UPDATEd supabase_migrations.schema_migrations on production to fake history linkage.
issues.root_cause_guess: Plan annotation/equip discovery never verifies a prescribed tool is actually installed before baking it into a blocking GATE; no tool-availability preflight in the build loop.

issues.title: Improvised baseline migration failed Supabase branch replay twice (MIGRATIONS_FAILED), burning three create/delete branch cycles
issues.evidence: 'status':'MIGRATIONS_FAILED' (twice) ... postgres log: 'relation organisation_members does not exist' ... second attempt: 'cannot use column reference in DEFAULT expression'
issues.what_happened: Hand-written baseline SQL had ordering bugs and a generated-column bug; two branches replayed to MIGRATIONS_FAILED before the third worked after manual fixes.
issues.root_cause_guess: Downstream of the missing Supabase CLI — no fallback path for generating a correct baseline.

issues.title: Checklist is static: prescribed merge_branch cutover and generate_typescript_types were renegotiated/skipped, but the original items were ticked anyway
issues.evidence: Checklist item: '...generate_typescript_types, merge_branch to production, delete_branch.' ... Claude: 'The cutover (checklist #16) is done: ... branch deleted, FE types skipped per your call.'
issues.what_happened: User redirected the cutover mechanism mid-build; promotion happened via apply_migration + custom script, types skipped. Build CLI offers only --check; no way to amend item text — boxes flipped on items describing actions that never occurred, corrupting the audit trail.
issues.root_cause_guess: Build verb set (--next/--check/--status) has no edit/annotate/skip-with-reason operation.

issues.title: User panic-interrupted a wired-MCP DDL call: blast radius of direct Supabase writes was not communicated up front
issues.evidence: Tool use rejected mid-DDL; user: 'is this makign changing on supbase? direcly because the code that may break the db'
issues.what_happened: Build loop issued apply_migration/execute_sql calls against production-adjacent infrastructure for many minutes before the user panicked, rejected a destructive step, and demanded explanation. Trust restored only via post-hoc explanation and AskUserQuestion gates.
issues.root_cause_guess: equip's blast-radius disclosure applies at install time only; nothing surfaces blast radius at USE time when checklist items drive a wired MCP into live infrastructure.

issues.title: Rejected apply_migration had actually already executed on the branch — re-run failed and state had to be forensically reconstructed
issues.evidence: Re-run error: 'Failed to apply database migration: ERROR: 42703: column scope of relation projects does not exist' ... Claude: 'the earlier rejection landed after the MCP call had already executed.'
issues.what_happened: User rejection arrived after the MCP DDL had run. Resuming the step errored; column-by-column introspection needed to discover the step had fully applied.
issues.root_cause_guess: Permission-prompt race with remote MCP writes: rejection is not a guarantee of non-execution; build flow assumes tick-state and DB-state stay in sync with approvals.

issues.title: Build loop shipped code ahead of schema: user hit a live 500 because backend+frontend migrated while the DB cutover item was held
issues.evidence: User pasted console TypeError 'Failed to fetch' ... Claude: 'asyncpg UndefinedColumnError: column is_default does not exist on GET /v1/brands → the new backend code is running against the un-migrated database.'
issues.what_happened: All code work completed at 26/27 with only DB promotion gated on approval. User ran the app and got 500s on every brands/projects call; emergency live cutover required.
issues.root_cause_guess: Plan ordering has no concept of a deploy-skew window; nothing warns that pausing before a gated cutover leaves the runtime broken.

issues.title: Equipment matching emits wrong subagent_type: a decision GATE and a pytest item both mapped to python-implementer
issues.evidence: --next for GATE item #0: 'agent: python-implementer, subagent_type: python-implementer' ... later: 'NEXT #13 -> python-implementer / ... add a pytest scan'
issues.what_happened: GATE (decision) item matched to python-implementer; explicit pytest work also matched to python-implementer (Claude overrode to python-tester). Matcher suggestions ignored whenever they mattered.
issues.root_cause_guess: Item-to-equipment matching is keyword/coarse with no notion of item kind (gate/decision vs implement vs test).

issues.title: Flat one-item-at-a-time loop doesn't fit atomic refactors: one agent did items #2–#12, then 11 boxes were batch-ticked with output suppressed
issues.evidence: Claude: 'I'll dispatch a single python-implementer for the non-test code (checklist #2–#12)' ... then a shell for-loop of --check with output to /dev/null
issues.what_happened: v0.15.0 skill mandates one-item-one-agent; the migration was an atomic compile unit, so Claude ran one mega-agent and batch-ticked 11 boxes, weakening verify-then-tick discipline.
issues.root_cause_guess: Flat checklists force a serial fiction; no 'check range/batch after shared verification' affordance (waves arrived later).

issues.title: Proposal grounding lacked environment wiring: Supabase project ref had to be scavenged from a different proposal, and op:// secrets were unresolvable
issues.evidence: 'op-read-unavailable' ... '[ERROR] account is not signed in' ... Claude: 'Is that the database your backend actually runs against...?'
issues.what_happened: Proposal said use the Supabase MCP but contained no project_id; Claude grepped a sibling proposal for refs, tried op read (not signed in), needed AskUserQuestion to identify the wired DB.
issues.root_cause_guess: Neither equip's wiring nor plan records concrete connection parameters (project refs, env resolution) alongside the wired MCP — every session re-derives them.

issues.title: Monorepo sibling (frontend) invisible to dummyindex: breaking cross-repo impact handled off-checklist with a generic agent
issues.evidence: Probe: '=== has its own .context? ===' (empty) ... dispatch: 'subagent_type: Frontend Developer'
issues.what_happened: Backend proposal broke the sibling frontend's API contracts; frontend had no .context/equipment, so the migration was improvised off-checklist with a generic agent. The user's later runtime error arose at exactly this seam.
issues.root_cause_guess: Index and equipment are single-repo scoped; plans cannot declare or track cross-repo consumers of a breaking change.

issues.title: SessionStart drift report is mtime-only and blind to the known docs-ahead-of-code split
issues.evidence: Drift hook flagged 4 incidentally-edited files while DECISIONS.md §12 carried the real, declared docs-vs-code split as a hand-written banner.
issues.what_happened: mtime drift flagged trivia and missed the largest known inconsistency; after the migration the same mechanism reported 'no feature drift'.
issues.root_cause_guess: Drift = mtime comparison only; no channel for declared/semantic drift.

issues.title: Checklist-prescribed get_advisors step produced a 92KB single-line result that overflowed the tool budget and broke first-pass parsing
issues.evidence: 'Error: result (92,666 characters across 1 line) exceeds maximum allowed tokens. Output has been saved to ...' ... follow-up parse: KeyError: 'lints'
issues.what_happened: Mandated advisor verification blew the token cap and spilled to file; first parse assumed wrong shape.
issues.root_cause_guess: Plan items mandating raw MCP calls don't anticipate oversized responses; no summarize/filter guidance.

summary: A /dummyindex-build run (skill v0.15.0) of a brand-centric DB+code migration completed 27/27 but exposed systemic gaps where dummyindex meets wired plugins: plans hardwired absent tools (Supabase CLI), blast radius was never surfaced at use time, a rejected MCP write had already executed, gated cutover created a deploy-skew window causing live 500s, the loop never truly closed (skill/CLI disagree on the closing command; Claude refused reconcile fearing re-clustering), and the unindexed frontend sibling was migrated off-checklist.
